races.race_name: Andersons - Kitchens Beautifully Built Around You Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Krissy
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has begun to tap into the abundant potential in her pedigree, with two wins at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) this spring; has also run well away from Musselburgh; did it very easily last Thursday and a 5lb penalty is unlikely to stop her if her fine form continues (she's 6lb well in).
runners.comment: Did it very easily at Musselburgh last Thursday; ahead of the handicapper under a penalty

runners.name: Runninsonofagun
runners.spotlight: Sole win (from 18 attempts) came back in August 2024 and he was down the field on this month's reappearance at Ayr; however, he may have needed that run and he's on a competitive mark on the pick of last season's form (including here); each-way chance if he's back on song.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but may have needed reappearance run and he's on a competitive mark

runners.name: Yafaarr
runners.spotlight: Best form has come on AW and he made it 0-6 on turf when fourth of eight, beaten about 4l, at Redcar (7f, good) three weeks ago; however, he kept on well and his last win came over 1m at Newcastle last October; has an each-way shout up in trip.
runners.comment: 0-6 on turf but kept on well over 7f at Redcar last time; each-way shout up in trip

runners.name: Lever Up
runners.spotlight: Made it 0-11 when sixth of 12 on last month's reappearance at Wetherby but it wasn't a bad performance and he could improve for it; his reduced mark appeals on last season's best form and he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: 0-11 but reduced mark appeals on last season's best form and he's not ruled out

runners.name: Pisanello
runners.spotlight: 9yo who is winless since June 2024 and is nothing like the force of old; however, his recent 4l fourth of eight at Hamilton (8.3f, good) wasn't devoid of promise and he's another 3lb lower today; not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: 9yo who is nothing like the force of old but his Hamilton fourth wasn't devoid of promise

runners.name: Condotti
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden but he ran some good races last season and reappears on a career-low mark; can give a good account if fully tuned up.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden but he ran some good races last season and reappears on a career-low mark

runners.name: Hostility
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden who can be difficult to settle and was well beaten at Ripon (1m, good) last month on sole previous run beyond 7f; however, he kept on strongly for seventh of 13 at Catterick (7f, good) last time, suggesting this trip might be worth a go, and his mark continues to fall; visor removed.
runners.comment: 0-16 and mixed messages regarding suitability of this new trip but it's worth a go

runners.name: Jewel Maker
runners.spotlight: 11yo who has plenty to prove on the evidence of this year's three runs; however, he's 6lb lower than when winning at Redcar last October and perhaps the return to Carlisle will trigger a revival, with his course form figures reading 4106231.
runners.comment: 11yo who has plenty to prove on recent evidence, but has a good mark & solid course record

runners.name: Golden Valour
runners.spotlight: 10yo who posted a fairly encouraging reappearance run at Ayr (7f, good to firm) last Wednesday, when rallying for fifth of eight; each-way possibilities if building on that run now back up in trip.
runners.comment: Fairly encouraging reappearance run at Ayr and each-way possibilities if building on it

races.race_name: Grandad Bill Whinnow EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Call Nicki
runners.spotlight: Improved upon her debut run at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) on May 2 when third there (5f, good) a fortnight ago, having attracted some market support; she's a leading player on the back of that latest effort.
runners.comment: Third at Thirsk on her second start and she's a leading player on the back of that effort

runners.name: May Bee In Profit
runners.spotlight: Close second at Beverley (5f, good to soft) on second start and third at Windsor (6f, good) last time, when shaping as though she would benefit from this return to 5f; every chance she'll be bang there.
runners.comment: Close second over 5f at Beverley then third over 6f at Windsor; in calculations back at 5f

runners.name: Tamam Star
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth on debut at Musselburgh (5f, good) last month, when he would have gone close with a clear passage; took a backwards step at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) six days later but it's possible that run came too soon; key player if leaving last time behind.
runners.comment: Promising fourth on debut at Musselburgh and his subsequent run may have come too soon

runners.name: Justice Time
runners.spotlight: 10,000euros yearling; third foal; dam once-raced half-sister to winners Orlaith (5f 2yo Listed), Florida Filly (5f 2yo) and Tammy Wynette (5f); it's likely this Inns Of Court newcomer will improve for the run.
runners.comment: 10,000euros yearling; dam from speedy family; likely she'll improve for this debut run

runners.name: Lincoln Warrior
runners.spotlight: 28,000euros yearling; fifth foal; Saxon Warrior half-brother to winners Alpheratz (1m; RPR 99) and Immutable (1m-1m4f Flat including 2yo, 98); dam unraced half-sister to St Leger runner-up Arrest and French Derby third Detain; market check advised on debut but it's possible he's best watched.
runners.comment: 28,000euros yearling with a useful pedigree, but possibly best watched on debut
runners.owner: Spg Property, Alfa Site & Graeme Curnow

runners.name: State Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Sent off just 9-2 for last month's debut at Redcar (5f, good to firm) but finished down the field; upset in stalls and withdrawn at Musselburgh nine days later (25-1 at the time); others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: Just 9-2 at Ripon on debut but down the field; 25-1 when withdrawn runner nine days later

runners.name: Brixworth Bandit
runners.spotlight: Showed pace and had a wide trip when well-beaten seventh of nine on this month's debut at Beverley (5f, good to soft); improvement is possible but he needs to take a sizeable step forward if he's to threaten today.
runners.comment: Improvement is possible on this second start but he needs to take a sizeable step forward

races.race_name: Andersons - Quality For The Home Handicap

runners.name: Ski Angel
runners.spotlight: Not at the top of her game at Hamilton (5f, good) a fortnight ago but before that she was an encouraging fourth on her comeback at Ayr (5f, good to firm); third in this last year and might not be far away if bouncing back.
runners.comment: Below best last time but encouraging comeback previously; third in this last year

runners.name: Get Up Everybody
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but has frequently run well, finishing third Pontefract (5f, good to soft) last month before a very close fourth at Redcar (6f, good); perhaps the first-time cheekpieces will eke out something extra; in the mix.
runners.comment: 0-11 but has frequently run well, including on both starts this season; in the mix

runners.name: Dandy Dinmont
runners.spotlight: He's inconsistent but he was back on song when winning at Beverley (5f, good to firm) this month; he's just 2lb higher today and is a leading player if backing up that latest performance.
runners.comment: Back on song when winning at Beverley this month; leading player if backing up that run

runners.name: Komorkis
runners.spotlight: Won a three-runner novice at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft) last September and very close fourth in a handicap at Nottingham (5f, good) last month on her seasonal/stable debut; below-par in a first-time hood (now left off) at Nottingham (5f, good) three weeks ago but she's not written off.
runners.comment: Very close fourth at Nottingham last month; below par there since but not written off

runners.name: Wee Mary
runners.spotlight: Sole win (from 23 attempts) came in AW classified in January; however, she's run well off this mark in turf handicaps on her last four starts (5f/6f, good/good to firm) and there's every chance she'll be bang there.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but running well on turf of late and every chance she'll be bang there

runners.name: Midnight Lir
runners.spotlight: Beaten 19 times since his last win in September 2023 and good runs have been scarce over the last year and a bit; however, it's possible he'll improve for this month's reappearance outing and he's not discounted off a handy mark in a reapplied tongue-tie and first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: His losing run continues but he's on a handy mark and is not discounted

runners.name: Digital
runners.spotlight: Four-time turf winner who won off today's mark at Newcastle (5f, AW) in February; however, he's finished down the field on his last four starts and a major turnaround is necessary.
runners.comment: Won off this mark at Newcastle in February but down the field on his last four starts
runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Lindoro
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on sole run this season (6f, AW) but showed promise last summer on his other two starts (6f/7f, good/good to soft); his dam was a useful 5f winner and he could appreciate the drop in trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: His dam was a useful 5f winner and he could appreciate the drop in trip on handicap debut

runners.name: Pop Star
runners.spotlight: His reduced mark appeals on form as recent as last September but he's struggled to get competitive on his seven runs since December.
runners.comment: Reduced mark appeals on form as recent as last September but he's struggled since

runners.name: Albegone
runners.spotlight: Close second at Newcastle (5f, AW) in February and likewise at Nottingham (5f, good) last month; however, soundly beaten on his three starts since and he needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Nottingham last month but soundly beaten on his three starts since
runners.owner: D B & Mrs C Lamplough & Partner

races.race_name: Andersons - Proudly Local Since 1888 Handicap

runners.name: Ganthorpe
runners.spotlight: Made it 0-9 when 4l seventh of 14 to Auspicious at Redcar (6f, good) three weeks ago; however, he's better than that, as evidenced by some positive runs earlier this year, and he has an each-way squeak if bouncing back.
runners.comment: Made it 0-9 when mid-division at Redcar but has an each-way squeak on pick of earlier form

runners.name: Auspicious
runners.spotlight: Mixed form since she won on nursery debut at Newcastle (6f, AW) last September but she returned to winning ways at Redcar (6f, good) three weeks ago; just 3lb higher today and holds strong claims for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Won at Redcar three weeks ago and just 3lb higher here; strong claims for in-form yard

runners.name: What A Tahoo
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but she ran her best race yet when third at Ripon (6f, good) 12 days ago; could be in the shake-up if confirming that promise.
runners.comment: 0-7 but ran her best race yet when third at Ripon 12 days ago; could be in the shake-up
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Only Dream Big
runners.spotlight: Improved upon her four 2yo runs when close fourth in handicap at Redcar (7f, good) on recent reappearance, when a stumble didn't help; firmly in calculations on the back of that effort.
runners.comment: Improved upon her 2yo form with close fourth at Redcar and she's firmly in calculations

runners.name: Kode Grey
runners.spotlight: Showed improved form and good pace when third on handicap debut at Ripon (6f, good) 12 days ago; on the shortlist today off the same mark.
runners.comment: Good pace when third on handicap debut at Ripon 12 days ago and he's on the shortlist

runners.name: Muddy Nora
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at big odds on her three 2yo starts and down the field at 22-1 on last month's handicap and seasonal debut at Nottingham (5f, good); improvement is necessary.
runners.comment: Down the field at 22-1 on last month's handicap/seasonal debut at Nottingham

runners.name: Saxon Gem
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure odds for all three starts (6f, good to firm/good) and hasn't achieved much; however, she now goes handicapping off a basement mark and she's from a family her trainer has done well with; could go well.
runners.comment: Hasn't achieved much but has basement opening mark and trainer has done well with family

runners.name: Ghadra
runners.spotlight: She has a useful pedigree (raced once for Al Shaqab) and she has a basement opening mark; however, she has shown very little and a transformation is needed in a first-time visor.
runners.comment: Basement opening mark but has shown very little; transformation needed in first-time visor

runners.name: Saxon Grace
runners.spotlight: 0-9 but her two runs for this stable haven't been without promise (6f/7f, AW) and 6f may prove to be her best trip; each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: 0-9 but not without promise in March for new yard and has each-way possibilities

races.race_name: andersonslimited.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Naturalia
runners.spotlight: Hasn't threatened over 1m1f on her last two starts but before those she reappeared with a good second at Catterick (1m4f, good) and is 3lb lower today; has won here and could have a big part to play back up in trip.
runners.comment: 1m4f runner-up on reappearance; lesser runs over 1m1f since; could have big part to play

runners.name: Himself
runners.spotlight: Won three in a row over 1m6f on AW early this year; he was returning from a three-month break when weakening over 2m at Catterick this month and drops back down in trip today, but he has to prove he's fully effective on turf.
runners.comment: Three AW wins early this year; tailed off on turf this month following three-month break

runners.name: Alpine Sierra
runners.spotlight: Won at Hamilton (1m3f, good) on May 3 and again had plenty in hand when following up at Musselburgh (1m4f, good) last week, when switched to front-running tactics; this thriving 8yo has strong claims under 4lb penalty.
runners.comment: Has won his last two with something to spare and this thriving 8yo has strong claims

runners.name: Ravenscraig Castle
runners.spotlight: Tailed off over hurdles last time but ran well in that code in March; he's on a handy mark on last year's best Flat form; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Ran well over hurdles two starts ago; handy mark on best 2025 Flat form; not ruled out
runners.owner: A Sinclair, I Jardine & V Renwick

runners.name: Taylormade Lad
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good to firm) last August and this lightly raced 4yo reappeared with a respectable third at Hamilton (1m3f, good) this month behind comfortable winner Alpine Sierra, who followed up; he's 11lb better off with his old rival this afternoon and could build on that latest effort.
runners.comment: C&D winner who reappeared with respectable third at Hamilton and could build on it

runners.name: Sweet Kiss
runners.spotlight: She won three times under Jake Dickson last August/September (1m2f, good to firm-good to soft) and may have needed her comeback run at Hamilton (1m3f, good) 12 days ago; however, she was well beaten and a swift and major turnaround is necessary.
runners.comment: Three wins last August/September but well beaten on her recent reappearance

runners.name: Fallons First
runners.spotlight: Tailed off when upped to 1m2f at Leicester last July and absent since; however, prior to that she kept on well for fourth on her handicap debut at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) and this very lightly raced 4yo remains one to be interested in for her top yard at this sort of trip.
runners.comment: Returns from absence but very lightly raced and could have potential at this sort of trip

races.race_name: Hoover - Performance That Lasts Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Singoura
runners.spotlight: 0-7 on turf but only beaten just over a length in her last two starts, the latest when second of eight at Redcar (7f, good) this month; that form is working out well and respected off 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Second at Redcar this month; that form is working out well and respected off 1lb higher

runners.name: Glorious Kitty
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 15th attempt when making all under Lewis Chalkley in an Ayr apprentice handicap (1m, good to firm) nine days ago; 4lb well in and dropping in trip shouldn't be a problem, but whether she will be allowed her own way again is debatable.
runners.comment: 4lb well in after recent Ayr win, but whether she will get her own way again is debatable

runners.name: King Sharja
runners.spotlight: 2lb well in having only just been denied at Southwell (7f, AW) eight days ago and 1lb lower than when successful at Beverley (7f, good to firm) last July; respected.
runners.comment: 2lb well in having only just lost out last time and 1lb below last winning mark; respected

runners.name: She's A Goldigger
runners.spotlight: 0-13 and best efforts have come on ground on the soft side of good; hard to recommend from out of the weights.
runners.comment: 0-13 and best efforts have come on ground on the soft side of good; hard to recommend

runners.name: No Nay Nevermind
runners.spotlight: Twice runner-up off a higher mark at Newcastle (7f, AW) late last year, but now 0-18; others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up off a higher mark at Newcastle late last year, but now 0-18
runners.owner: S Jones, L Donaldson & K Elliott

runners.name: Pallas Lord
runners.spotlight: Handy sort who is 1-1 here and ran well returned to turf when beaten a short head and a neck into third of eight at Hamilton (1m, good) a fortnight ago; ground faster than good probably not ideal, though.
runners.comment: 1-1 here and a close third at Hamilton last time, but fast ground probably not ideal

runners.name: Sir David
runners.spotlight: Sole success came over C&D from 4lb higher last August when trained by Stella Barclay, but regressive in the meantime including in three starts since returning for this yard last month; best watched.
runners.comment: Sole success came over C&D from 4lb higher last August, but regressive since

runners.name: Valentine Catcher
runners.spotlight: Still 3lb lower than when second of eight at Pontefract (6f, good) last month, but losing run up to 15 and he does seem best at shorter.
runners.comment: Losing run up to 15 and he does seems best at shorter

runners.name: Finn Ironside
runners.spotlight: Still 1lb below his last winning mark and ran well when third of 16 at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) last month (a nose ahead of Singoura); drawn widest, but still high on list.
runners.comment: Still 1lb below his last winning mark and ran well when third at Doncaster last month

runners.name: Rory's Gold
runners.spotlight: Placed once in six starts for Paul Attwater with his best efforts coming on the AW; picked up for 1,000gns in March and drops in trip on stable debut after 11 months off; market informative.
runners.comment: 0-6; drops in trip on stable debut after 11 months off; market informative

races.race_name: Hoover - Performance That Lasts Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Emerald Army
runners.spotlight: Still 5lb below his last winning mark and was having only his third start for the yard when beaten a neck by a much better-fancied stablemate at Catterick (7f, good) eight days ago; already due a 2lb rise for that and no surprise if he steps up again; major player.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck by a much better-fancied stablemate at Catterick eight days ago; 2lb well in

runners.name: The Gay Blade
runners.spotlight: Handy sort who has been successful three times on the AW this year; has won four times on turf, but was found to have bled at Ayr last time; others more convincing.
runners.comment: Successful three times on the AW this year; was found to have bled at Ayr last time

runners.name: Ziggy's Angel
runners.spotlight: Made a successful handicap debut at Newcastle (7f, AW) last September, but there was a heavy defeat on his return to turf at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) five weeks ago.
runners.comment: Newcastle AW winner in September; heavy defeat on return to turf last time

runners.name: Kings Merchant
runners.spotlight: Won four times in 2024, but losing run up to 14 despite a tumbling mark; fair effort when fourth of 13 at Catterick (7f, good) 18 days ago, though, and the winner has gone in again since; each way claims.
runners.comment: Losing run up to 14 despite a tumbling mark, but a fair fourth at Catterick 18 days ago

runners.name: Jamaican Storm
runners.spotlight: 1-19 and his best form has come on the AW, albeit he didn't run very well at Southwell last time and his previous fourth of 13 at Thirsk (1m, good) was better; frame material again based on that effort.
runners.comment: Frame material based on his fourth at Thirsk on his penultimate start

runners.name: Carolus Magnus
runners.spotlight: Not the most prolific, but he was returning from 134 days off when beaten just a neck at Musselburgh (7f, good) last month; respected off 1lb higher if coming on from that.
runners.comment: Returning from 134 days off when beaten just a neck at Musselburgh last month; respected

runners.name: Doon The Glen
runners.spotlight: Beaten around 4l by Jet Warrior when second of ten at Ayr (6f, good to firm) nine days ago and a 5lb pull is probably nowhere near enough; only win in 31 starts came in a 0-50 classified event.
runners.comment: Held by Jet Warrior on latest Ayr running and only win came in a 0-50 classified event

runners.name: El Pinto
runners.spotlight: 0-7; showed plenty of ability in both starts on soft ground as a 2yo, but has regressed in an abbreviated career since; best watched.
runners.comment: Showed plenty of ability in both starts on soft ground as a 2yo, but has regressed since

races.race_name: LSL Racing Horse Sales Syndication Leasing Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: V Power
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m6f AW winner at Wolverhampton this year, both times under Zoe Lewis; did not enjoy run of the race when well-held third there 18 days ago and also has form over this C&D; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Dual 1m6f AW winner this year; also suited by this C&D; probably won't be far away

runners.name: Endofastorm
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who came second twice at Bath (11.6f/1m2f, firm) last month; subsequent lesser effort there is probably best overlooked (slowly away after anticipating start), and she can give this a good shot.
runners.comment: Front-runner; second twice at Bath last month and recent run there is best overlooked

runners.name: Oasis Sunrise
runners.spotlight: In-form filly who has been placed twice (1m2f/1m) since last month's comfortable win at Bath (1m, firm); lost out only by a short head when back at Bath a week ago and is 1lb ahead of the handicapper for this first attempt at 1m4f; has big role to play if stamina holds out.
runners.comment: In good form over 1m/1m2f this spring; 1lb ahead of the handicapper for first 1m4f attempt

runners.name: Sapphire Sirocco
runners.spotlight: 1-13 when trained by Pat Phelan, the win gained off basement mark at Lingfield (2m, AW) in November; makes stable debut after 142-day absence; worth a glance in the betting but others have more obvious claims.
runners.comment: Gained only win over 2m on AW; makes stable debut after 142-day absence

runners.name: Stage Show
runners.spotlight: This year's hurdling form is uninspiring but he held on gamely to cause 66-1 shock at Salisbury a fortnight ago, his first Flat run since 2024; it's hard to tell whether he'll be in the same mood again here but he's only 2lb higher and this is a slight drop in grade.
runners.comment: Not easy to predict but held on gamely to cause 66-1 surprise at Salisbury this month

runners.name: Trojan Truth
runners.spotlight: Won three consecutive times in refitted blinkers last June/July, most recently off today's mark over C&D (good to firm), but made a low-key reappearance three weeks ago and needs that run to have brought him on.
runners.comment: Three wins (one over C&D) last June/July; made low-key reappearance this month

runners.name: Paps Of Jura
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all eight starts; seemingly aided by first-time hood when respectable fourth of seven at Bath (1m2f, firm) last month but needs to find something extra over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Respectable fourth of seven at Bath (1m2f) in April but yet to be placed after eight runs

runners.name: Sea Of Charm
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with 1m4f AW win in February, but didn't fire when back on turf this month and her tendency to race too freely is always a worry; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Has won on AW this year but didn't fire when back on turf this month; others preferred

races.race_name: Finli Live Your Best Financial Life Handicap

runners.name: Regulus Black
runners.spotlight: Unable to justify warm favouritism but kept on well for third when upped to 1m2f for recent handicap debut at Beverley (good to firm); probably still has potential; high on the list.
runners.comment: Kept on for third when upped to 1m2f for recent handicap debut; still has potential

runners.name: Gnocchi
runners.spotlight: Safely held in two AW handicaps this year (1m/1m2f) and now 0-6 overall; eased in grade for this turf return, though, and might be boosted by the first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Safely held in two AW handicaps this year; eased in grade with cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Dash Of Class
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; placed in first-time hood at Southwell (1m, AW) last month but managed only fifth of seven when favourite for turf debut at Bath (1m, firm) three weeks later; upped in trip here; still needs to prove herself on grass.
runners.comment: Placed on AW last month but was disappointing favourite on turf debut; up in trip

runners.name: Time To Sparkle
runners.spotlight: Opened account at sixth attempt, with narrow AW success at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in March, and was very respectable third when upped to 1m2f at Yarmouth (good to firm) last month; holds each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Very respectable third when upped to 1m2f last month and holds each-way claims at least

runners.name: Leia Antilles
runners.spotlight: Launched career with three low-key runs, most recently when eighth in Lingfield maiden (1m, AW) on last month's seasonal debut; not bred to benefit from today's step up in trip but, nevertheless, improvement is possible in handicaps.
runners.comment: Launched career with three low-key runs but improvement is possible in handicaps

runners.name: Echo Valley
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only four runs but was soundly beaten when 80-1 for recent handicap debut at Bath (1m2f, firm).
runners.comment: Soundly beaten when 80-1 for recent handicap debut at Bath (1m2f)

runners.name: No Drama Mama
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all five AW starts, most recently when last of eight in 9.4f handicap in March; needs combination of switch to turf and first-time cheekpieces to prompt significant improvement.
runners.comment: Unplaced all five AW starts; moves to turf with cheekpieces added

runners.name: Go For Gold
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing more than minor promise in three novice/maiden races (7f-1m) in the autumn, but ought to get more competitive in low-grade middle-distance handicaps this year; market may guide.
runners.comment: Safely held on all three 2yo starts but ought to get more competitive in handicaps

races.race_name: Bill Lewtons 80th Birthday Novice Stakes

runners.name: Alfaraz
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on both 2yo starts (1m/8.5f) and not beaten far when sixth of ten in what has predictably turned out to be a strong Newbury novice (1m2f, good) last month, despite racing too freely in the lead; will be hard to beat if first-time hood helps him to relax here.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when sixth in strong Newbury novice last month; should be hard to beat

runners.name: Echo Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Probably below the form of his two 2yo runs when fourth of 11 on seasonal reappearance at Newbury (1m2f, good) a fortnight ago but that was still a pretty good effort; won't be far away if first-time tongue-tie has any positive effect.
runners.comment: Probably below his 2yo form when fourth at Newbury this month, but still considered

runners.name: El Nay
runners.spotlight: Out of a useful and well-connected 1m winner; placed on all three starts, most recently when second of five in quite valuable Chester maiden (10.3f, good) this month; does not appear to be progressing and ideally needs to learn to settle better, but ought to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Does not appear to be progressing but has been placed on all three starts; in the mix

runners.name: Kinetic Impact
runners.spotlight: 90,000gns yearling; second foal; dam well beaten 1m2f AW in France only start, half-sister to French 1m2f Listed winner Kapour; in good hands but has tough standard to reach on debut.
runners.comment: Out of a half-sister to a French Listed winner; has tough standard to reach on debut

runners.name: Madame Dragon
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam multiple point winner, placed over hurdles and fences; on paper, she's a very unlikely candidate to make a winning debut in a good Flat novice such as this.
runners.comment: Out of a multiple point winner; very unlikely to make winning debut in this company

runners.name: Blue Hercules
runners.spotlight: Last of eight in 1m3f Newbury maiden on last month's debut but belied triple-digit odds when fourth of nine there (1m2f, good) a fortnight ago and will have each-way claims if he can make any further progress.
runners.comment: Belied big odds when fourth at Newbury this month and has each-way claims

runners.name: Strong Man Tom
runners.spotlight: Struggled in two bumpers this spring and also when 250-1 for C&D maiden on recent Flat debut.
runners.comment: Struggled in both bumpers and in C&D maiden on recent Flat debut

runners.name: Prophet Squeeze
runners.spotlight: A lowly rated maiden over jumps; makes stable/Flat debut after 275-day absence; impossible to recommend.
runners.comment: A lowly rated maiden over jumps; makes stable/Flat debut after 275-day absence

races.race_name: 3 Day Pass £30 DragonBet WRF Handicap

runners.name: Cayman Tai
runners.spotlight: Has dropped a fair way down the weights over the last year or so, but looked back in prime form when scooting clear to win readily by 4l at Southwell (5f, AW) last week; also suited by good to firm turf and is 4lb ahead of the handicapper under his 5lb penalty.
runners.comment: Seemingly back in prime form when scooting clear on AW last week; 4lb well in

runners.name: In The City
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who dropped out as though something was amiss on recent seasonal debut; consistent in maiden/novice races last year, though, and can pose a potent threat off this mark if back on song for handicap debut.
runners.comment: 0-6; flopped on seasonal return but makes handicap debut from a good mark

runners.name: Over Spiced
runners.spotlight: Looked as good as ever when holding on to win from the front at Bath (5f, good) this month; also suited by this C&D but managed only fifth on her next two starts and might remain vulnerble off a mark this high.
runners.comment: As good as ever when winning this month but looks a shade vulnerable off this mark

runners.name: So Smart
runners.spotlight: Yet to build upon his pleasing seasonal debut in April, but didn't run badly when third of six over C&D (good to soft) eight days ago; 1lb lower now and Ashley Lewis claims his useful 5lb; considered.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly over C&D last week and Ashley Lewis claims his useful 5lb today

runners.name: On Edge
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent 6yo who gained last season's win at Bath (5.7f, good to firm) in September; subsequent defeat on heavy ground there can be overlooked but he makes his reappearance from a fairly tough mark.
runners.comment: Largely consistent 8yo; makes seasonal debut from fairly tough mark

races.race_name: UB40 Live After Racing 24th July Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Thanos
runners.spotlight: Came good at seventh attempt, drawing clear to win comfortably by 6l in first-time cheekpieces at Lingfield (7f, good) ten days ago; 4lb well in under 6lb penalty here; obvious claims if suited by the new trip.
runners.comment: Won by 6l in new cheekpieces last week (7f); 4lb well in under 6lb penalty here

runners.name: Alice's Influence
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who dead-heated for first in first-time visor at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) last month and took another step forward when very close second at Bath (1m, good) 25 days ago; respected in current mood.
runners.comment: Dead-heated for first in new visor at Wetherby last month and went close on latest outing

runners.name: Mooj
runners.spotlight: Placed at Leicester (6f, good) on debut; her next two 6f runs were underwhelming but her pedigree offers hope that she'll be suited by the step up in trip on this handicap debut; improvement possible.
runners.comment: Two lesser 6f runs followed her promising debut; still open to improvement in handicaps

runners.name: Diamond Aura
runners.spotlight: Showed promise on debut in October (6f, AW) but tailed off when fitted with cheekpieces (retained) for 7f AW nursery in December on latest outing; upped in trip for turf debut; leap of faith required to support her.
runners.comment: Tailed off in AW nursery in December; leap of faith required to support her

runners.name: Karmacy
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on all five starts, most recently when seventh of eight on this month's stable debut at Lingfield (7f, AW); back up in trip here; needs to find improvement from somewhere.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten all five starts, most recently on this month's stable debut

runners.name: Violet Goldsmith
runners.spotlight: Still unexposed after only four runs but could not justify her prominence in the betting when towards rear throughout last month's seasonal/handicap debut at Southwell (1m, AW) and arrives here with a point to prove; cheekpieces removed after one use.
runners.comment: Still unexposed but flopped on seasonal/handicap debut; arrives here with a point to prove

runners.name: Lihou Lady
runners.spotlight: Showed at least some promise in all three 7f AW novice runs, the latest in March; needs to improve on this handicap/turf debut but it could be significant that Pat Cosgrave (who partnered stablemate Alice's Influence last time) keeps the ride on this filly.
runners.comment: Showed some promise in three 7f AW novices; up in trip for turf/handicap debut

races.race_name: LSL Racing Horse Sales Syndication Leasing Maiden Stakes

runners.name: Food For Thought
runners.spotlight: Bettered mildly encouraging 2yo debut when clear second of five behind very useful rival at Yarmouth (1m, good) in October; this drop back in trip is not an obvious move, but she should have more improvement to come this season and her Irish Oaks entry suggests she's held in some regard.
runners.comment: Second to very useful rival on second 2yo start; holds Irish Oaks entry
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Guitar Solo
runners.spotlight: Safely held on this month's debut but put in pretty good front-running shift when sixth of 15 at Newbury (7f, good) a fortnight ago; has good chance to collect at least a minor prize here.
runners.comment: Good front-running shift when sixth in big field at Newbury this month; place claims
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Quisana
runners.spotlight: First living foal from unraced but well-connected mare; outran 40-1 odds when keeping on for promising fourth of ten at Windsor (1m, good) 11 days ago; ought to play a significant role if this shorter trip suits.
runners.comment: Kept on for promising fourth of ten on recent Windsor debut (1m); likely contender

runners.name: Ingemar
runners.spotlight: 42,000euros yearling; ninth foal; half-brother to 1m Group 3 winner Indian Blessing and two useful French midde-distance winners; dam useful 1m2f/1m4f winner; already gelded; market helpful on debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to a 1m Group 3 winner; already gelded; market helpful on debut

runners.name: Diane Chasseresse
runners.spotlight: Made fairly encouraging debut when sixth of nine at Lingfield (7f, AW) this month but needs improvement.
runners.comment: Made fairly encouraging debut on AW this month but needs improvement

runners.name: Brocklesby Bill
runners.spotlight: Showed his inexperience when a down-the-field 200-1 shot on recent debut at Newbury (1m2f, good; hooded, as today); drops in trip and remains best watched.
runners.comment: A down-the-field 200-1 shot on debut at Newbury two weeks ago; drops from 1m2f

runners.name: Watercraft
runners.spotlight: Did not live up to pedigree when safely held over 7f on both 2yo starts; can probably improve this year but others have much more obvious claims.
runners.comment: Did not live up to his pedigree when safely held over 7f on both 2yo starts

races.race_name: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9-11th October Handicap

runners.name: Too Much Trevor
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 29 starts but bettered low-key seasonal debut when close second from out of the weights over C&D (good to soft) last week and is effectively 4lb lower today; also suited by faster ground; obvious contender if in similar form.
runners.comment: 0-29 but went close over C&D last week and is effectively 4lb lower today

runners.name: Romanovich
runners.spotlight: Won off reduced mark at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in March and has remained in good form since; only faded out of contention in closing stages when fifth over C&D (good to soft; behind Too Much Trevor and Connie's Rose) last week and ought to be better suited by today's faster ground; one to consider.
runners.comment: Only faded out of contention in closing stages over C&D last week; one to consider
runners.owner: Mrs S M Maine & Roger Gould

runners.name: Forever My Prince
runners.spotlight: Made encouraging seasonal/stable debut when fifth of 15 in higher-grade race at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) in March, but two lesser efforts have followed (7f/6f) and she'd appeal more on ground slower than good.
runners.comment: Ran quite well on seasonal debut but two lesser efforts follow; may prefer slower ground

runners.name: Ajrad
runners.spotlight: Usually ridden patiently; step back in right direction when keeping-on fifth at Kempton (7f, AW) this month, but his three turf wins in 2025 were gained over 1m and he'll probably need a strong pace to aim at over this trip.
runners.comment: Latest run was step back in right direction but he needs a good pace to aim at over 7f

runners.name: Connie's Rose
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who is much better known as a sprinter, but won over C&D in 2022 and looked suited by return to this trip when close third here (good to soft; just behind Too Much Trevor) last week; back in action before 1lb nudge takes effect and will be fine on the faster ground; respected.
runners.comment: Close third over C&D last week; back in action before 1lb nudge takes effect; respected

runners.name: King Of The Dance
runners.spotlight: Slightly disappointing when well-held fourth on this month's turf return at Bath (1m), but was in good form on AW beforehand (7f-8.6f) and is well suited by this C&D; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Slightly disappointing on recent turf return but was in good form on AW beforehand

runners.name: Vape
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner (once here) last summer; also suited by 7f but we haven't seen him since a disappointing AW run in September and he probably doesn't have much margin for error off current mark.
runners.comment: Dual 6f winner last summer; fine over 7f but absent since poor AW run in September

runners.name: Redredrobin
runners.spotlight: Gained three of her eight wins over this C&D and has slipped to a good mark, but ended last season on a low note and last month's reappearance run was also disappointing; needs to get back in the groove.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; well beaten on seasonal debut; needs to get back in the groove

runners.name: Oviedo
runners.spotlight: Very useful over middle-distances in his prime but was badly out of sorts on AW when last seen in the winter; drops back in trip and sports first-time cheekieces; considered if lively in the betting.
runners.comment: Badly out of sorts on AW when last seen in winter; drops back in trip with cheekpieces on

runners.name: Northcliff
runners.spotlight: Now 16lb lower than when winning at Ascot (6f, good to firm; also suited by 7f) on stable debut last spring, but finished well down the field in all four AW sprints since December and returns to turf with lots to prove.
runners.comment: Well down the field on last four AW starts; returns to turf with a lot to prove

races.race_name: Weatherbys Flat Horses To Follow Handicap

runners.name: Grey Horizon
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a fine spell on AW during the winter, winning four consecutive handicaps at 6f across January and February; came back to form when returned to 6f with third of 14 at Windsor (good to firm) on Saturday; same mark (due to go up 2lb) and this is less competitive.
runners.comment: Back in form at 6f when third of 14 on Saturday; same mark (due up 2lb) and strong claims

runners.name: Tenzi
runners.spotlight: Went fairly close on her debut at Goodwood (6f, good) in June and just held on to open her account at Southwell (7f, AW) in March; handicap debut wasn't so good when back at 6f (AW) one month ago but it's still early days.
runners.comment: Last month's handicap debut (6f, AW) was a little underwhelming but it's still early days

runners.name: Argy Bhaji
runners.spotlight: Fairly consistent when placed in three AW qualifying starts and it was an encouraging handicap/turf debut when second of seven at Yarmouth (again 6f, good to firm) last month; probably capable of better and he's respected.
runners.comment: Encouraging handicap debut second (6f, good to firm) last month; unexposed and feared

runners.name: Jane Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden but she went close at Southwell (AW) in March and finished on the heels of the principals when fourth of five in a blanket finish at Chepstow (also 5f, good to soft) last Thursday; more exposed than some of these but she stays 6f and can't be ruled out, albeit yard may have better prospects with Grey Horizon.
runners.comment: Fourth of five in blanket finish over 5f last Thursday; stays 6f and can't be ruled out

runners.name: Nifty
runners.spotlight: Promising juvenile who won Chelmsford maiden (AW; second start) in June and fairly valuable Salisbury novice (again 6f, good to firm) three weeks later; signed off last term with solid handicap third but worryingly she finished last in both starts this spring; drops in grade with blinkers given a whirl and needs to prove she's trained on.
runners.comment: Won twice as 2yo but last in two runs at three; blinkers tried; worry she's not trained on

runners.name: King Of Chaos
runners.spotlight: Made all to win Lingfield maiden (7f, AW) in February but his subsequent handicap form doesn't bare similar inspection, including when beating one home at Newmarket (6f, good) a fortnight ago; falling mark the straw to clutch at.
runners.comment: 7f AW maiden winner but hasn't shone in subsequent handicaps; mark is edging down though

races.race_name: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk EBF Restricted Maiden (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)

runners.name: Terry O
runners.spotlight: February foal who did well in a messy debut when fourth at Bath (5f; Sayidah Ardad behind) this month and backed that up when fifth of 12 at Windsor (6f, also good) 11 days ago; again had some excuses last time and he has the makings of a nice type, particularly when things go a little more smoothly.
runners.comment: Did well despite messy debut and backed that up 11 days ago; big player with a clean shot

runners.name: High Calibre
runners.spotlight: 82,000gns February foal by Lucky Vega whose priced dropped to 42,000gns as a yearling; dam German 1m 2yo winner, half-sister to winners Grocer Jack (German/Italian 1m2f Group 3) and Ghislaine (German 7f Listed), out of German 1m3f Group 3 winner from family of Gold Cup winner Fame And Glory; likely to do better over further in time but is still considered from respected yard.
runners.comment: 82,000gns February foal; 42,000gns yearling; considered from respected yard

runners.name: Extended Order
runners.spotlight: Already-gelded April (fourth) foal by Without Parole; dam unplaced 1m1f-13.5f in France, closely related to 1m2f-1m4f winner Swiftsure, half-sister to US 1m3f Grade 2 winner Expansion, out of French 10.5f Group 3 winner; heed any market clues on this debut.
runners.comment: April foal by Without Parole; dam unplaced; heed any market clues on this debut

runners.name: Sovereign Beach
runners.spotlight: 62,000gns breeze-up 2yo; half-sister to four winners including So Majestic (Listed-placed 5f; RPR 97); dam 1m AW winner (88), half-sister to 6f/7f Group 3 winner Majestic Queen; 11-2 for novice at Ripon (6f, good) yesterday but always behind and beaten 11l.
runners.comment: 11-2 for novice at Ripon (6f, good) yesterday but always behind and beaten 11l

runners.name: Sayidah Ardad
runners.spotlight: March (first) foal; £4,000 yearling; looked to need the experience when seventh of ten at Bath (5f, good; Terry O fourth) 25 days ago; should do better.
runners.comment: March foal; £4,000 yearling; looked to need debut at Bath 25 days ago; should do better

runners.name: C'Est Lui
runners.spotlight: Early-May foal who was far from disgraced at 100-1 on his debut at Bath in late April but still looked green when weakening into midfield at Redcar (6f, good) 11 days ago; still early days.
runners.comment: Fair debut but still green when midfield at Redcar 11 days ago; still early days

races.race_name: Download The Fairplay App Now "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)

runners.name: Kimeko Glory
runners.spotlight: Won three times at 1m2f (good/good to firm) in her time with Mark Walford and Brian Toomey last year; encouraging start at 1m4f for this yard and first-time blinkers worked a little too well when a still-respectable fourth of 15 at Newbury (good) a fortnight ago; can do better wearing that aid for the second time.
runners.comment: Fair fourth when lit up by first-time blinkers latest; can do better in that aid this time

runners.name: Gallant Lion
runners.spotlight: 1m2f win here (good to firm) kicked off a four-timer in 2023 for Tony Carroll but he took a while to rediscover his level before scoring at Chepstow (1m4f, good; first-time blinkers) for this yard one year ago; raced mainly over further without success since and latest start over 1m can be written off; handy mark and he's not discounted on just his second visit to Brighton.
runners.comment: 1m2f win here and last scored at this trip; handy mark and he's not discounted back here

runners.name: Golden Circet
runners.spotlight: Three-time turf winner, including when just fending off OJ Lifestyle at Epsom (extended 1m, good; first-time visor) in July; following wind surgery he stuck at it from the rear when fifth over 1m2f (AW) last month and has run well over this trip previously; mark fine and he needs considering.
runners.comment: Stuck at it from the rear when fifth over 1m2f last month; trip/mark fine; considered
runners.owner: W Trenchard, A Coupland, Cheam Marketng

runners.name: Rogue Impact
runners.spotlight: Highly tried after winning Lingfield maiden (AW) in April last year and he bounced back from a swing at the Derby when going close at Thirsk (also 1m4f, good) in July; that form would make him a handicap snip but it's been a real struggle since and blinkers are now reached for; only considered if the market warms to him.
runners.comment: Out of form and blinkers are now reached for; only considered if the market warms to him

runners.name: Oj Lifestyle
runners.spotlight: Three wins here (1m, good to firm), most recently in October, immediately raise interest and he stuck at it when third over 1m2f on his final start of last term; quiet reappearance in February probably best ignored and he's worth another go at this trip (tried once two years ago); respected.
runners.comment: Three wins here (1m) & stuck at it when third over 1m2f on last start of 2025; respected

runners.name: Maasai Mara
runners.spotlight: Regressed over hurdles last year but there was some encouragement with a hood deployed when fourth of seven at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW) on his Flat return last month; blinkers (retained) were back on when a pace bias and wide draw did for him at Chester three weeks ago; not entirely ruled out.
runners.comment: Some encouragement penultimate start and latest can be forgiven; not entirely ruled out
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late K W Bell

runners.name: Mister Daydream
runners.spotlight: Went back-to-back (1m2f/1m3f on a sound surface) for Charlie Johnston last spring and is now 8lb below the latter winning mark after some lesser efforts for that yard; picked up for 1,000gns in October and is another to keep an eye on in the betting on this stable debut.
runners.comment: Went back-to-back (1m2f/1m3f on a sound surface) last spring; watch market on stable debut

runners.name: Abando
runners.spotlight: Ex-German runner who is another to have contested a Derby, in his case when beaten less than 10l in the Hamburg version in July; nowhere near that form in two starts for Marco Botti (including once in cheekpieces) and similar story on both outings for this yard; gelding operation, handicap debut and a mark in freefall put him top of the market watchlist, though.
runners.comment: Group form in Germany; gelding operation/h'cap debut/mark in freefall so watch the market

races.race_name: Watch Live Racing On fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Joycean Way
runners.spotlight: Blinkered when he hit a run of second spots at 7f and 1m last summer, including here (soft; acts on good to firm) in September; had previously looked unlucky on a couple of those occasions and the blinkers were absent when never figuring at Lingfield ten days ago; might do better with that aid now refitted and he's dangerously handicapped; watch the market.
runners.comment: Run of second spots at 7f/1m last summer, including here; blinkers back on; watch market
runners.owner: J Harley, T Jacobs, G Moore

runners.name: Unknown Journey
runners.spotlight: 0-23 and after going close in a Lingfield classified in February he's been way off that in three subsequent starts, including when tailed off last time.
runners.comment: 0-23 and way below par on last three starts, including when tailed off last time

runners.name: Aim For The Bull
runners.spotlight: Dual AW scorer who picked up the second of those wins at Kempton (1m) in March; two solid AW runs followed and he should be fine back on turf, having placed in a course classified (1m, good to firm) in July; player.
runners.comment: Won second AW race (1m) when scoring in March; solid since and has course form; player

runners.name: Celtic John
runners.spotlight: Only start here produced a second over C&D (good to soft) in October and he got off the mark in a Lingfield classified (1m, AW) in January; not quite matched that in three starts since and yet to prove he's effective on quick turf; still can't be ruled out in a moderate contest.
runners.comment: 2nd only start over C&D; won (1m, AW) in January; fast turf a query but he's not dismissed

runners.name: Celebrating Ethel
runners.spotlight: Landed her second win on turf when picking up 1m2f course classified in August; similar trip and grade when winning at Wolverhampton (AW) in March but two lesser efforts followed; drops back in distance.
runners.comment: Won on AW in March but two lesser efforts followed; drops back in distance

runners.name: Havana Mojito
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner (7f/1m) who disappointed in this race last year when chasing a Brighton hat-trick; bounced back when runner-up at Epsom next time but he 's not been in the same form since, including when fifth at Leicester on Monday; body of work at this venue says he can never be dismissed, however, and first-time blinkers might reignite the fire.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner (7f/1m) but struggling since August; blinkers might fire him up

runners.name: Manton Road
runners.spotlight: Won four times on AW in the first three months of last year for Gay Kelleway but left that yard under a cloud and hasn't built on encouraging first go for this stable in March; work to do on his return to turf.
runners.comment: Encouraging first go for this stable wasn't built on; work to do on his return to turf

runners.name: Banksman
runners.spotlight: Finished second both previous starts over C&D and also won two classified events when trained by Jack Channon; well beaten in two starts on the Flat since joining this stable, however, and was pulled up over hurdles 40 days ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Well beaten both starts on the Flat since joining this yard; pulled up over hurdles latest

runners.name: Weston Court
runners.spotlight: Cheekpieces were on for both of his wins in the summer of 2024 but he's generally struggled in that aid since and trailed the field on three of his last four starts; newly fitted visor needs to prompt a big revival.
runners.comment: Has been struggling for a good while and newly fitted visor needs to prompt a big revival

runners.name: Lady Of The Isles
runners.spotlight: Signed off last year with best effort yet when fourth of 15 at Leicester (1m2f, good; first-time hood) in October; two runs in blinkers this month haven't seen her return in the same form but the handicapper's starting to relent and this is winnable.
runners.comment: Hasn't returned at her best but the handicapper's starting to relent and this is winnable

runners.name: Rovinia
runners.spotlight: Started well for this yard and her good run of form led to breakthrough win at Kempton (7f, AW) in October; struggled over a variety of trips on her last five starts, however, including from this career-low mark at Bath on Tuesday.
runners.comment: Struggled over a variety of trips on last five starts, including on Tuesday

races.race_name: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap

runners.name: Dion Baker
runners.spotlight: Four wins include one over C&D (good) in May 2024; hasn't scored since August of that year but he's now 20lb lower and latest fourth of 13 at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) was a solid effort despite hanging; should enhance creditable Brighton form figures of 21424.
runners.comment: C&D form figures of 21424 and ran with credit at Doncaster latest; high up the list

runners.name: Valsharah
runners.spotlight: 23-race maiden who has tried various headgear (including combinations) since joining this yard but it was only today's tongue-tie he sported when close second at Kempton (6f, AW) in March; career-low mark and he's a contender if subsequent efforts can be forgiven.
runners.comment: Just the tongue-tie when 6f AW second in March; chance if subsequent efforts are forgiven

runners.name: Kondratiev Wave
runners.spotlight: In the veteran stage now but the old man's been giving younger legs plenty of trouble this year, dead-heating at Lingfield (7f, AW) in February and placed on another five occasions; still in good heart judged on latest second at Bath (1m, firm) and this dual C&D winner can go well again.
runners.comment: In the veteran stage but still going well; dual C&D winner and he looks the solid option

runners.name: Havana Joy
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; yet to score but she was placed in her first five starts (6f/7f, AW) last year; 196-day break preceded a poor effort in November, however, and wind surgery hasn't improved matters judged on two runs this year; tongue-tie and blinkers thrown into the mixer and she's dangerously handicapped if rediscovering her mojo on this turf debut.
runners.comment: Started well but has lost her form; tongue-tie and blinkers need to spark a big revival

runners.name: My Boy Harry
runners.spotlight: Mostly campaigned on AW but he's now 2-9 on turf having landed C&D classified (good to firm) in June and Lingfield handicap (7f, good) last Tuesday; took advantage of a golden highway that day but did well to pick his way through from the rear and he's respected under a 5lb penalty (1lb well in).
runners.comment: Won latest (7f, good); possibly flattered but warrants respect under penalty; 1lb well in

runners.name: Dalmally
runners.spotlight: Won a 2yo course maiden (good to soft) in 2024 but flopped on his return visit when favourite for a handicap (also 1m, good to firm; first-time blinkers) last September; left Hughie Morrison after another lesser effort and that pattern has continued in three starts for this yard; hopes probably pinned on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Course winner but seems to have lost his way; hopes probably pinned on drop back in trip

runners.name: Zu Run
runners.spotlight: First visit here proved a winning one when taking handicap over this trip (good to firm) last May; flopped on his second go one month later and only bits and pieces of form since; career-low mark and cheekpieces (0-3) make a return after two-year absence.
runners.comment: C&D winner last May but only pieces of form since; cheekpieces return off career-low mark

races.race_name: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap (Brighton Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Dark Sun
runners.spotlight: 0-17 record includes a near miss over 7f last summer and two over 6f early this year; Windsor 6f fourth three weeks ago confirms her place claims but it's not surprising that connections now reach for cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 0-17; place claims but it's not surprising that connections now reach for cheekpieces

runners.name: Lynwood Lad
runners.spotlight: Won a 5.5f classified on the first of his two visits to Brighton (last summer) but ran poorly in the other; latest start was easily his best in 2026 but that was in March and he's inconsistent.
runners.comment: Won here last June; latest start (March) was easily his best in 2026 but he's inconsistent

runners.name: On Key
runners.spotlight: 0-19 and modest form nowadays; also down the weights and followed three competitive 7f efforts on AW with a fair show in big field on turf last time, but this first visit to Brighton needs to spark something extra from him.
runners.comment: 0-19; competitive but this first visit to Brighton needs to spark something extra from him

runners.name: Top Biller
runners.spotlight: Most of his best efforts have come from the front but he just raced in touch when second at Yarmouth (6f) on latest outing, his best show this year; well handicapped if judged on peak 2025 efforts.
runners.comment: Best show this year was last time; well handicapped if judged on peak 2025 efforts

runners.name: Port Hedland
runners.spotlight: The extended 5f at Brighton (good to firm) last May brought his only win from 27 races; his most competitive shows since were on AW this March but he was second favourite on return to turf at Bath last Friday, only to be hampered; not entirely dismissed.
runners.comment: Sole win (27 races) was here; second favourite on return to turf last Friday but hampered

runners.name: Thanks Dad
runners.spotlight: Fourth on all four appearances at Brighton; 29 races overall, with both his wins on Southwell AW last March/September; thrown in on that form but he was out of form this winter and on recent return to turf after five months off; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Fourth on all four races at Brighton; 29 overall, with both wins on Southwell AW

runners.name: Secret Handsheikh
runners.spotlight: The latest of his 11 wins was last August off 5lb higher; no win over this far since 2021 but he goes well at this track and, despite recent form, can't be comfortably ruled out.
runners.comment: No win over this far since 2021 but he goes well here; can't be comfortably ruled out

runners.name: Bated Breeze
runners.spotlight: 0-18, with his solitary second over 7f on soft; ended 2025 with two respectable thirds in October, including over 7f here on good to firm, but first time out has seen two of his best shows, so he needs a second look today.
runners.comment: 0-18 but first time out has seen two of his best shows, so he needs a second look today

runners.name: Lil Wade
runners.spotlight: This is only his third race on turf but he finished last and second last in the others; none of his 2025 or 2026 form reads well enough and his 0-33 overall record is probably the bottom line.
runners.comment: No 2025 or 2026 form reads well enough and 0-33 overall record is probably the bottom line

runners.name: Henry Tudor
runners.spotlight: Well backed when firmly on top in a 6f classified event at Chelmsford (AW) in February but that's the only time he's not been well held; usual accessories were left off that day, as they were in a handicap in March on his only start since; form case depends on that one run and this is turf debut.
runners.comment: 6f classified win (AW) in February is the only time he's not been well held; turf debut

runners.name: Starsong
runners.spotlight: Fared well from low draws at this track last year, including for two wins, and she's been close up at other tracks on two of her four starts this year; drawn high this time but has to be seriously considered.
runners.comment: Fared well at this track last year, including for two wins; seriously considered

runners.name: Voodoo Ray
runners.spotlight: The first four of his five wins were at Brighton but no success anywhere since May 2024; discouraging record from autumn 2024 onwards.
runners.comment: No success anywhere since May 2024; discouraging record from autumn 2024 onwards

runners.name: Neptune Legend
runners.spotlight: Third run back with Tony Carroll when taking advantage of his reduced mark at Bath (5.7f) last Friday and he did it in good style; that gives him a big chance under just a 4lb penalty but he's run with credit at Brighton only once from eight visits.
runners.comment: Big chance judged on Bath win last Friday but his record at Brighton is a worry

races.race_name: Streamline Taxis 202020 Dash From The Stalls Handicap

runners.name: Kento
runners.spotlight: All three wins at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) including two this year; 0-9 on turf, though, and an easy lead seems unlikely, especially from a wide draw; stable also runs Wedgewood.
runners.comment: 0-9 on turf and an easy lead seems unlikely, especially from a wide draw

runners.name: Barnsnape Boy
runners.spotlight: Doesn't have a great record fresh so this month's Windsor reappearance isn't hard to forgive; won easily from 2lb lower over C&D (good) last August having made all, but similar tactics won't be easy in this field, especially from the widest stall.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D last August, but similar tactics won't be easy in this field

runners.name: Coolagh Magic
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is 9-46 on the AW and 0-21 on grass, but he has become dangerously well handicapped on the pick of his turf form last year; should find this race is run to suit.
runners.comment: 0-21 on grass but has become dangerously well handicapped on the pick of his turf form
runners.owner: Lady Emma Oshea And A Carson

runners.name: Dreambird Dolly
runners.spotlight: 0-17, but she has been placed in three of her five starts over C&D so can be given an each-way shout; new headgear combination.
runners.comment: 0-17, but placed in three of her five starts over C&D; new headgear combination
runners.owner: T Beeches, C Stone, J Gallagher

runners.name: Rainbow Sorbet
runners.spotlight: Third in his only outing for Michael O'Callaghan at Gowran Park (1m, good) a year ago, but nothing in two starts at Lingfield (6f, AW) since returning for this yard in March; makes handicap debut in a first-time visor; probably best watched unless market suggests otherwise; stable also runs Almavillalobas.
runners.comment: Makes handicap debut in a first-time visor; best watched unless market suggests otherwise

runners.name: Battle Point
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame a few times over C&D, but a record of 1-49 doesn't make him an appealing proposition.
runners.comment: Record of 1-49 doesn't make him an appealing proposition

runners.name: Almavillalobas
runners.spotlight: Has shown very little in nine starts from 5f-11.4f; visor removed; makes no appeal.
runners.comment: Has shown very little in nine starts from 5f-11.4f; makes no appeal
runners.owner: C Seeney, S Holden, Mildean , M Robinson

runners.name: Apple's Angel
runners.spotlight: 3-14 on the AW for Simon Dow and 0-7 on turf; may need this first start for her new trainer after 256 days off and is probably better over a bit further now.
runners.comment: May need this first start for her new trainer after 256 days off
runners.owner: P And Mrs L Rowe And John Turner

runners.name: Coast
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who ran well when fourth of 13 at Bath (5.7f, good to firm) a week ago, but her losing run is up to 26 and she's unlikely to get her own way in this field.
runners.comment: Losing run up to 26 and she's unlikely to get her own way in this field

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Money Back If 2nd To SP Fav Handicap

runners.name: This Guy
runners.spotlight: Maiden was runner-up three times in a row at Dundalk during the winter; not as good in recent months; midfield in 6f Curragh handicap on latest but this is an ease in grade; Colin Keane takes over; high draw not ideal.
runners.comment: Still a maiden; midfield at Curragh last time; this is an ease in grade; draw not ideal

runners.name: Plushy
runners.spotlight: Record is 0-10 but has run well in defeat; placed on first two starts in 5f maidens last year at Bellewstown (good) and Cork (good to firm); excuses for Naas flop on latest as fly leapt the stalls; contender eased in grade.
runners.comment: Enjoys good; excuses for Naas flop as fly leapt the stalls; contender eased in grade
runners.owner: Derek Murray/Poetry In Motion Syndicate/Patrick McGuinness

runners.name: Baggot Street
runners.spotlight: Ran well in defeat on multiple occasions for Charlie Johnston at 6f/7f; sported first-time blinkers when running out a surprise 33-1 winner on stable/seasonal debut at Navan (6f, yielding) 13 days ago; should go well again.
runners.comment: Surprise 33-1 winner on stable/seasonal debut at Navan (6f, yielding); should go well

runners.name: Mini Cotai
runners.spotlight: Modest form in good-ground turf maidens last year; shaped well over 6f at Dundalk in January when third; back from short break when giving way late on to finish fourth, beaten 1.5l in first turf handicap at Cork (5f, soft); 2lb wrong.
runners.comment: Some fair efforts including in first turf handicap latest but that was on soft; 2lb wrong

runners.name: Hooves Your Daddy
runners.spotlight: Fair effort when fifth in Naas maiden in August (6f, good); struggled in following three runs but back from a break when runner-up at Dundalk last month (5f; had Love Bomb, Plushy and Lady Landhort behind); entitled to respect back on turf.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Dundalk last month (5f; had Love Bomb, Plushy and Lady Landhort behind)

runners.name: Lady Landhort
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in 6f Dundalk handicap in March off 49; came up short off revised mark twice since, one place behind Hooves Your Daddy over 5f at Dundalk on latest; 3lb lower on first turf handicap; in the mix.
runners.comment: One place behind Hooves Your Daddy over 5f at Dundalk; 3lb lower in first turf handicap
runners.owner: T J Flanagan & Ronan Macken & Shane McSweeney & S O'Malley

runners.name: Love Bomb
runners.spotlight: Won C&D handicap (yielding) last September off 3lb higher; yet to really fire this term but takes a big drop in class after a poor run at the Curragh 10 days ago; drawn widest of all.
runners.comment: Won C&D handicap (yielding) last September off 3lb higher; drawn widest of all

runners.name: Spirit Above
runners.spotlight: Showed no signs of winning potential in maidens or on handicap debut at Dundalk (6f) on latest; can only be watched down in trip in first turf handicap.
runners.comment: Showed no signs of winning potential in maidens or on handicap debut at Dundalk

runners.name: Spiced Gold
runners.spotlight: Three undistinguished runs as a 2yo; tailed off behind Baggot Street on handicap/seasonal debut at Navan 13 days ago; best watched.
runners.comment: Tailed off behind Baggot Street on handicap/seasonal debut at Navan; best watched

runners.name: Cashel Blue
runners.spotlight: Failed to fire in three maidens at 5f/6f on yielding/soft ground, the latest of those at Navan 13 days ago when beaten 11.5l into fifth; likely improver now handicapping; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much in three maidens; likely improver now handicapping; worth a market check

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Money Back Meetings Every Day Fillies & Mares Maiden

runners.name: What About Us
runners.spotlight: Bought for 160,000euros last May; two encouraging opening efforts when hitting the frame in 7f Dundalk maidens late last year; found only the Group 1-entered Glyfada too good at Killarney (1m, good) on return 17 days ago; the one to beat.
runners.comment: Found only the Group 1-entered Glyfada too good at Killarney (1m, good) on return

runners.name: Justiciar
runners.spotlight: Top-rated in the field with a mark of 83; promise on Curragh debut (7f, good) last June when 1.5l third behind Composing (peak rating 110); disappointing since, including on handicap debut at the Curragh (7f) on latest; headgear needs to bring out improvement.
runners.comment: Repeat of debut run behind Composing would be enough but hasn't gone on from that effort

runners.name: Wearing Stars
runners.spotlight: Twice placed over 6f on AW late last year; fourth in 5f Cork maiden on reappearance; never in contention on 6f handicap debut at Naas last month; others are more likely for win purposes.
runners.comment: Never in contention at Naas last month; others are more likely for win purposes

runners.name: Cactus
runners.spotlight: Well exposed daughter of Wootton Bassett out of a mare by Galileo; regressive tail end of last year; fourth of 24 in 6f Curragh maiden (soft) on return; hard to be confident despite the pedigree, connections and being Aidan O'Brien's sole runner on the card.
runners.comment: Well exposed; hard to be confident despite pedigree, connections and yard's sole runner

runners.name: Hasita
runners.spotlight: Contested two very strong Curragh maidens last May/June as a juvenile; returned with eyecatching never-nearer third of 15 at Cork (6f, soft); wasn't given a hard time and every chance with Ben Coen back aboard if she handles quicker ground.
runners.comment: Eyecatching return run at Cork; every chance with Ben Coen back aboard if she handles good
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Ruby Vega
runners.spotlight: Lucky Vega filly; 19,000euros foal; half-sister to 5f winner; dam unplaced, half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Came From The Dark, out of Group-placed 7f winning half-sister to 6f Group 1 winner Pipalong; watch.
runners.comment: Lucky Vega filly; 19,000euros foal; half-sister to 5f winner; dam unplaced; watch

runners.name: Croi Gra
runners.spotlight: Respectable debut when third at a big price in 1m Gowran maiden dominated by market leaders; raced far too keenly and built up big early advantage before weakening on next start at Navan (1m2f); drop in trip may help.
runners.comment: Respectable debut; raced far too keenly over 1m2f at Navan last time; drop in trip a help
runners.owner: Gary Devlin & Kevin Osborne & Richard J Fitzsimons

runners.name: Dandymaria
runners.spotlight: Dandy Man filly; 23,000euros yearling; sister to Listed-placed 5f-6.5f winner Whistle And Flute (inc 2yo; RPR 106); dam unraced half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 2 winner Agnes Stewart and 1m5f Listed winner Sorrel; watch betting.
runners.comment: Dandy Man filly; 23,000euros yearling; sister to Listed-placed 5f-6.5f winner (inc 2yo)

runners.name: Sioux The Boss
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly; 70,000euros yearling; half-sister to four winners inc Measure Of Time (French 1m1f-1m6f inc 2yo/Listed; RPR 102); dam unraced sister to 1m2f/1m4f Listed Flat winner and smart jumper Stage Affair.
runners.comment: Sioux Nation filly; 70,000euros yearling; half-sister to four winners inc Measure Of Time
runners.owner: John G Hennessy & C Acheson & Mrs John Harrington

runners.name: Youse Raven Mad
runners.spotlight: Raven's Pass filly; 16,000euros yearling; half-sister to winners High Fibre and Granary Queen; dam 7.4f 2yo/1m winner (72), half-sister to Australian 1m4f Group 3 winner Caravan Rolls On; best watched.
runners.comment: Cost 16,000euros; half-sister to winners High Fibre and Granary Queen; best watched

runners.name: Rebel Wave
runners.spotlight: New Bay filly's price rose from 110,000gns as a yearling to 280,000euros as a foal; looked in need of the run when fifth of eight on Naas debut (1m, good) 12 days ago; should strip fitter now.
runners.comment: Expensive purchase; looked in need of the run when fifth of eight on 1m Naas debut

runners.name: Miss Moore Cen
runners.spotlight: Very capable young trainer but will need to step up a lot on midfield finish at the Curragh (6f) on debut when behind Cactus.
runners.comment: Will need to step up a lot on midfield finish at the Curragh on debut when behind Cactus

runners.name: Due In June
runners.spotlight: Made little impression in 7.5f maiden claimer at Roscommon on debut 11 days ago; hooded.
runners.comment: Made little impression in 7.5f maiden claimer at Roscommon on debut 11 days ago; hooded

runners.name: Courtlough Gula
runners.spotlight: Sent off 200-1 for 7f Naas debut last September and failed to beat a single rival home.
runners.comment: Sent off 200-1 for 7f Naas debut last September and failed to beat a single rival home
runners.owner: Paul M Butler & James Patrick Byrne

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Extra Places Every Day Handicap

runners.name: Eichan San
runners.spotlight: Now 0-8 but has ability judged on his keeping-on fourth of 21 at the Curragh on penultimate (6f, soft); the drop to 5f was a bad move last time (outpaced) but can't rule out a big run over this trip.
runners.comment: Player judged on penultimate Curragh run; drop to 5f was a bad move; this trip okay

runners.name: Burford Beauty
runners.spotlight: First reserve; just fair form in handicaps to date, including fifth over C&D; can get upset in the stalls.
runners.comment: Just fair form in handicaps, including 5th over C&D; can get upset in the stalls; reserve

runners.name: Thaloria
runners.spotlight: Stepped up markedly on maiden runs when scoring on handicap debut at Dundalk in January over 7f; not as effective on turf and was completely tailed off in 1m Curragh handicap (good) on Sunday; eased in grade but others preferred.
runners.comment: Not as effective on turf and was completely tailed off in 1m Curragh handicap on Sunday

runners.name: Bay Of Dreams
runners.spotlight: Won twice for James Ferguson last year, including on turf at Nottingham; tailed off in two starts for this yard, behind third-placed Faoladh at Leopardstown on latest.
runners.comment: Tailed off in two starts for this yard, behind third-placed Faoladh at Leopardstown latest

runners.name: Minaun View
runners.spotlight: Third runner for yard; maiden after 15 starts; one of her better runs when 3l third of 15 at Limerick (1m, yielding; one place behind Amemri) on penultimate; best work late on when fifth of 14 at Roscommon (7.5f, good) on latest; place shout.
runners.comment: Maiden after 15 starts; running well without really threatening; place shout

runners.name: Lohengrin
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Laytown maiden last September; poor winter form at Dundalk; brought down at Bellewstown on return; in rear at Navan and Roscommon; Colin Keane is 0-8 on him; looks vulnerable.
runners.comment: In rear at Navan and Roscommon; Colin Keane is 0-8 on him; looks vulnerable

runners.name: Shoot To Kill
runners.spotlight: Two wins last year on good ground for different trainers, including 7.5f Roscommon handicap off 80; now 10lb lower plus talented claimer takes off another 5lb; got his favoured ground when eyecatching third of 14 at Roscommon from tough draw 18 days ago; big chance.
runners.comment: Thrown in on last year's form and eyecatcher last time; has his ground; big chance

runners.name: Charming Whisper
runners.spotlight: Won five times when trained in Britain by Philip McBride; all those wins came on ground with good in the description; slowly away and always behind at the Curragh on latest; new headgear.
runners.comment: Player judged on 5 wins in Britain on decent ground but out of sorts at the Curragh

runners.name: Una Matata
runners.spotlight: First turf run since September last year; excellent record on AW of late, winning four of her last 10 starts, three at this trip; one place behind Thaloria when third in 7f claimer there when last seen; tough mark back on grass.
runners.comment: Excellent record on AW of late, winning four of her last 10 starts; tough turf mark
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Mark McGuckin & Claire Wylie

runners.name: Faoladh
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a good spell last summer, winning at Leopardstown (1m, good) off 1lb lower; fair efforts in defeat this year, including third at Leopardstown a fortnight ago; likely to be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: Won Leopardstown handicap on good (1m) last year; in decent nick at present; player

runners.name: Brains
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; seventh in this race last year; not beaten far on last two runs; in the mix.
runners.comment: Seventh in this race last year; not beaten far on last two runs; in the mix; reserve

runners.name: Land Of The Giants
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; in good form on turf recently, with a breakthrough win at Ayr (1m1f, good to firm) off 63 at the start of the month; fairly treated back on home soil but below his best in two starts since, including Dundalk claimer on Tuesday.
runners.comment: Handles fast ground, winning over 1m1f at Ayr three starts back; not as good since
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Nigel O'Hare & Philip Smith & Bart O'Sullivan

runners.name: Amemri
runners.spotlight: Back to form when recording a tenth career win at Dundalk in March; has won three times on turf and finished runner-up of 15 at Limerick (1m, yielding) on latest when just in front of Minaun View; player.
runners.comment: 10 career wins; runner-up of 15 at Limerick on latest when just in front of Minaun View

runners.name: Dynamic Force
runners.spotlight: Speedy in his early days, winning three times over 5f for Robert Cowell; runner-up when sent off favourite for this race (good) last year; 8lb lower now but on a long losing sequence and weakened at Leopardstown on return.
runners.comment: Runner-up when sent off favourite for this race last year; 8lb lower but out of form

runners.name: Goldsmith
