runners.spotlight: Third reserve; five AW wins when trained in Britain; mostly below form for this yard.
runners.comment: Five AW wins when trained in Britain; mostly below form for this yard; reserve

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap

runners.name: Beau Army
runners.spotlight: Has won over 6f/7f at Dundalk and runner-up over 1m there last month; yet to run a good race on turf, including when tailed off at Gowran (1m) last month.
runners.comment: Prefers AW; yet to run a good race on turf, including when tailed off at Gowran (1m)

runners.name: Queen Of Newark
runners.spotlight: Weak form last season and never involved first two handicap starts over 5f; career-best when third in Roscommon maiden claimer (7.5f, good to yielding) 11 days ago but will need to pull out more back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Third in Roscommon maiden claimer 11 days ago; needs to pull out more back in a handicap

runners.name: Andosky
runners.spotlight: Best effort when runner-up in Sligo nursery (6f, good to yielding) last year off 64; soundly beaten all three starts at trips ranging from 5f-7f this term; has to improve but handicapper's grip is loosening.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten all three starts at trips ranging from 5f-7f this term; has to improve

runners.name: Bullet Bourbon
runners.spotlight: Maiden went closest when chasing home a progressive type over 6f at Dundalk in April; slowly away over 7f at Gowran next time; another slow break over 6f at Navan on latest; others more likely.
runners.comment: Might be better on AW; can be very slowly away and 6f might be her optimum
runners.owner: David Daly & Gerard Shevlin & D Battersby

runners.name: Imnotleavinyou
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; placed in Naas nursery last term and finished runner-up all three outings this season at 6f-1m, each time staying on late on ground with an ease; quicker ground possibly not ideal but still shortlisted.
runners.comment: Runner-up all three outings this season at 6f-1m but all on softer than this

runners.name: Fontaine Khaled
runners.spotlight: Nice run in good-ground Curragh maiden last year; well held first two handicap starts but not beaten too far in much better race than this at Naas (6f) last month; wouldn't be too quick to dismiss her.
runners.comment: Not beaten too far in much better race than this at Naas (6f) last month; player
runners.owner: Dan Kiely & C O'Neill & Apollo Thoroughbreds

runners.name: Honey Dale
runners.spotlight: Not a bad run when fifth at Gowran last month (7f, soft); failed to see out 1m there last time on better ground; easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Okay run on soft at Gowran on penultimate but well below that on better ground latest

runners.name: Sand Art
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three maidens and no sign of improvement for switch to handicaps at Dundalk last time.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three maidens and no sign of improvement for switch to handicaps at Dundalk

runners.name: Thistle Cross
runners.spotlight: Couple of modest AW efforts late last year; completely tailed off on turf/seasonal debut; easily overlooked despite now handicapping.
runners.comment: Completely tailed off on turf/seasonal debut; easily overlooked despite now handicapping

runners.name: Dervo Annie
runners.spotlight: Long odds and soundly beaten in trio of maidens at Navan/Dundalk over 5f-7f late last year; never threatened when seventh of 13 on 1m Bellewstown handicap debut last month; booking of Colin Keane a plus.
runners.comment: Seventh of 13 on 1m Bellewstown handicap debut last month; booking of Colin Keane a plus

runners.name: Meriden
runners.spotlight: Never threatened in trio of maidens at 6f-1m on soft/AW; got going late when sixth of 13 on handicap debut at Ballinrobe (9.5f, good to yielding) three weeks ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Got going late when sixth of 13 on handicap debut at Ballinrobe (9.5f); others preferred

runners.name: Sweet Baby Zou
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in three qualifying runs at 6f-1m2f on testing ground; looked in need of further when seventh of 14 at Roscommon (7.5f, good) on handicap debut but can't rule out for top yard.
runners.comment: Looked in need of further when seventh of 14 at Roscommon (7.5f, good) on handicap debut

runners.name: Another Nice'day
runners.spotlight: Respectable fifth at this course over 5f in her first handicap in September, staying on after taking a hold in first-time cheekpieces; failed to see out this trip at Dundalk next time; gelded ahead of return.
runners.comment: Good 5th here over 5f on handicap debut; didn't see out 7f at Dundalk; gelded for return

runners.name: Stonyfalls
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in four 7f maidens; has a more realistic opportunity now on handicap debut; market can reveal expectations after 203 days off.
runners.comment: More realistic opportunity now on handicap debut; market can reveal expectations

races.race_name: His Majesty's Plate (Listed Race)

runners.name: Carmers
runners.spotlight: Won his first three races around this time last year, notably 1m6f Group 2 Queen's Vase; better again in defeat when second in 1m4f Great Voltigeur and fifth to Scandinavia in St Leger; not quite at same level on last month's seasonal return behind same rival in Navan Listed; visor now replaces blinkers.
runners.comment: Below best on last month's seasonal return but big player on last season's form

runners.name: Dallas Star
runners.spotlight: Won 1m2f Group 3 Ballysax Stakes in 2024 (1m2f, heavy); last win was at Dundalk early last year, having faced some stiff tasks since, career-best when front-running second to ready winner Scandinavia in Navan Listed last month; not as good behind same rival since under similar tactics in Group 3 Saval Beg; bit to find on ratings and step up to 2m looks a big ask.
runners.comment: Front-runner has a bit to find on ratings and step up to 2m looks a big ask

runners.name: Tennessee Stud
runners.spotlight: Group 1-winning 2yo made the frame last year in Epsom/Curragh Derbies and career-best when winning the Group 2 Prix Chaudenay (1m7f, very soft) at Longchamp in October; disappointing when last seen in Group 2 Riyadh contest in January, fading tamely on quick ground after forcing much of pace; demands plenty of respect on overall form.
runners.comment: Ground may have been too quick in Riyadh; demands plenty of respect on overall form

runners.name: Alba Chiara
runners.spotlight: All her best form has been here, notably winning 2m2f Ulster Cesarewitch last September off a lowly mark of 63; never sighted on last weekend's Curragh comeback run and tried blinkered here; proven stayer and likes the track but loads to find on ratings.
runners.comment: 2m2f Ulster Cesarewitch win last September but loads to find on ratings
runners.owner: Mrs Tracey O'Hare & D W Sloan & William Jones

runners.name: Mont St Michel
runners.spotlight: Ex-Ballydoyle, for whom he won over 2m1f at Killarney last summer; brief spell hurdling for new connections before finishing last on both Flat outings, latest in Group 3 Saval Beg; out of depth again here.
runners.comment: Finished last in Group 3 Saval Beg; out of depth again here
runners.owner: Patrick John O'Brien & J Carthy & M G Hogan

runners.name: Layfayette
runners.spotlight: Winner of ten races, most recently last month's seasonal return in good-class Curragh conditions event, coming late in testing conditions (first attempt at 1m6f); never able to land a blow later same month on quicker ground in Saval Beg; first attempt at 2m.
runners.comment: Stretched his stamina out this term, decent chance of staying this longer trip now

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Handicap

runners.name: Mephisto
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick in handicap hurdles last summer before losing his way; decent run at Dundalk last month off 8lb higher AW mark so has to be of interest back on the turf (stamina proven).
runners.comment: Decent run at Dundalk last month off 8lb higher AW mark so has to be of interest

runners.name: Jurality
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden under both codes but running well of late, most recently staying-on third over 1m5f at Leopardstown (good); shortlisted.
runners.comment: Recent Leopardstown third ensures he makes the shortlist here

runners.name: Al What
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden didn't get beyond the first (swerved and unseated) at Ballinrobe on Tuesday; previous Roscommon Flat third under today's rider was a solid run so don't rule out.
runners.comment: Unseated over hurdles at Ballinrobe on Tuesday; previous Flat run decent so don't rule out

runners.name: Tassarolo
runners.spotlight: Three wins last year, two over hurdles and 1m4f Killarney Flat handicap off 59; recent Flat return was encouraging at Leopardstown although remains unproven over this extended trip.
runners.comment: Recent Flat return encouraging although remains unproven over this extended trip

runners.name: Maxwell Smart
runners.spotlight: Sole win came here last September (1m5f, good) but numerous placings include this term at Sligo; not much luck in running at Leopardstown on latest so remains of interest, especially given his record here.
runners.comment: Won here last September and excuses on latest; one to consider
runners.owner: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Synd'/D O'Leary/J McNally

runners.name: Lisnadill
runners.spotlight: Placed twice for Andy Oliver last summer, including a narrow defeat at Naas (10.5f, good); after a long layoff, well held on both starts for current yard this term on much softer ground (headgear combination tried on latest now ditched); return to better ground could see an improvement.
runners.comment: Return to better ground could see an improvement

runners.name: Fine Print
runners.spotlight: AW winner went close at Wolverhampton (1m6f) 18 days ago; due to run over hurdles at Wexford on Wednesday.
runners.comment: AW winner went close at Wolverhampton (1m6f) 18 days ago

runners.name: Bang Po
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; nothing of any substance since 2023 Killarney win; 9lb wrong.
runners.comment: Nothing of any substance since 2023 Killarney win; 9lb wrong; reserve

runners.name: Miners Cabin
runners.spotlight: Quite lightly raced 4yo never sighted on recent handicap debut at Leopardstown; good bit to find with a couple of today's rivals on that run.
runners.comment: Never sighted on recent handicap debut at Leopardstown; good bit to find

runners.name: Velvet And Vine
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden had been running okay in 1m4f AW handicaps last winter; back from a break with stamina to prove.
runners.comment: AW 1m4f form last winter; back from a break with stamina to prove

runners.name: Timandi
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden returned from AW campaign when never counting over inadequate 1m at the Curragh in March (heavy); given a break since and ground more in her favour here; stamina unproven.
runners.comment: Heavy ground against her when last seen in March; ground to suit now but stamina unproven
runners.owner: Mrs N K Griffin & T Brosnan

runners.name: Edge Of Darkness
runners.spotlight: First reserve; four-time winner over 1m4f back in the day in Britain; nothing like that sort of form for current yard, mark rapidly descending of late.
runners.comment: 4-time 1m4f winner in Britain; nothing like that sort of form of late; reserve

runners.name: Our Lucky Lady
runners.spotlight: Better hurdler, winning Sligo maiden (2m2f, good to yielding) in October, just okay run in handicap at Punchestown festival on latest; precious little of note on the Flat last year, tried hooded now on return and may improve.
runners.comment: Proven a better hurdler since last running on the Flat; hood tried and may improve

runners.name: Synchronize
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden showed some ability in maiden hurdles but has been poor in handicaps; of limited appeal back on the Flat.
runners.comment: Mild hurdles promise at times but of limited appeal back on the Flat

runners.name: Pons Aelius
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner at up to 2m in Britain but yet to hit any sort of form for current yard; career-low mark and tongue-tie tried so don't rule out.
runners.comment: Yet to hit any sort of form for current yard; career-low mark and tongue-tie tried

runners.name: Among The Pines
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden wasn't sighted in three maidens last year and last of 17 on handicap debut at Navan in October (upset in stalls), trained then by Michael Grassick; big step up in trip for seasonal/yard debut and best watched.
runners.comment: Nothing last year; big step up in trip for seasonal/yard debut and best watched

runners.name: Sioux Grit
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; 0-18 and absent two years; 8lb wrong here.
runners.comment: Second reserve; 0-18 and absent two years; 8lb wrong here

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Daily Racing Rewards Handicap

runners.name: Misty Cove
runners.spotlight: Couple of okay AW runs over the winter and reappeared for winning turf/handicap debut early this month, making most over this trip at Sligo (yielding-to-soft); 3lb rise okay, should go well again.
runners.comment: Sligo winner should go well again off 3lb higher

runners.name: Grey Intentions
runners.spotlight: Didn't really count on recent handicap debut over a mile at Cork; longer trip may suit but lots to find with Harmani on that run; cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: Longer trip may suit but lots to find with Harmani on Cork run
runners.owner: Thomas J Quinn & Brian P Whelan

runners.name: Harmani
runners.spotlight: Twice beaten favourite in maidens following promising 2yo debut; more like it on handicap debut early this month when good second in Cork apprentice event over a mile; shapes as though this longer trip will suit.
runners.comment: Recent Cork handicap debut second shaped as though this longer trip will suit

runners.name: Pierre Grosse
runners.spotlight: Improved for step up in trip when placed in 1m2f Leopardstown handicap on last month's return; similar RPR when clear second to a ready winner in Naas rated contest early this month; place claims again.
runners.comment: Two good runs of late at this trip; place claims again

runners.name: Chestnut Palace
runners.spotlight: Just mild promise in three maiden runs before Leopardstown handicap debut earlier this month, seemingly not getting home over 1m4f; drop back in trip to suit but may not reverse form with Eniac.
runners.comment: Drop back in trip to suit but may not reverse form with Eniac on Leopardstown run

runners.name: Eniac
runners.spotlight: Opening handicap effort earlier this month offered plenty of encouragement, coming home strongly after not clear run to finish fourth behind a progressive type over 1m4f; drop in trip may not be ideal but major player nonetheless with likely more to offer and rider claims a valuable 5lb off top weight.
runners.comment: Eyecatching handicap debut; drop in trip may not be ideal but likely more to offer

runners.name: Another Day Done
runners.spotlight: Nicely improved in first-time visor to handsomely win 1m Dundalk handicap in January (beating a subsequent winner); not clear run and hung right over same C&D 10 days later; not certain he stayed today's trip at Leopardstown last time (over 2l off Pierre Grosse at level); gelded since and visor back on (replacing cheekpieces worn latest).
runners.comment: Not certain he stayed this trip last time and bit to find with Pierre Grosse

runners.name: Chapel Lane
runners.spotlight: Improved on latter of three maiden runs in second half of last season, rallying third in first-time visor (left off here) over a mile at Thurles; good chance he'll improve further at this longer trip.
runners.comment: Encouraging final 2yo effort; good chance he'll improve further at this longer trip

runners.name: Lord Aus
runners.spotlight: Slowly away from the gates in all three maidens this spring when never featuring, upset in stalls latest; potential improver upped in trip for handicap debut but may need more time.
runners.comment: Potential improver upped in trip for handicap debut but may need more time

runners.name: Oust
runners.spotlight: Drop back to this trip at Roscommon recently seemed to suit when third under a positive ride; more likely needed.
runners.comment: Roscommon third over this trip encouraging; more likely needed though

runners.name: Rapide Vega
runners.spotlight: Has improved in each of three runs, notably last month's Gowran return when fifth of 15 (1m, soft); upped in trip now for handicap debut off a high enough mark.
runners.comment: Upped in trip now for handicap debut off a high enough mark

runners.name: Vantage Code
runners.spotlight: Big price for all three maiden starts at two (some promise second occasion at Thurles), and again when never sighted on seasonal return in Curragh handicap last month; return to better ground may suit, any market support worth heeding.
runners.comment: Return to better ground may suit, any market support worth heeding

runners.name: Sutton Hoo
runners.spotlight: Nicely improved for step up to today's trip at Dundalk in February, clear with second in winning maiden; seemingly likes a sound surface so could have more to offer here back on turf for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Dundalk maiden winner at the trip; could have more to offer back on turf for handicap bow

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Best Odds Guaranteed Maiden

runners.name: Teologia
runners.spotlight: Definite step forward on latest when coming from good bit back to chase home a stablemate in Ballinrobe claimer over just shy of this trip, despite running green; cheekpieces now added and major player.
runners.comment: Ballinrobe claiming second augurs well; cheekpieces now added and major player

runners.name: Maxminelli
runners.spotlight: Definite step forward in first-time tongue-tie in similarly-rated Ballinrobe maiden early this month, putting it up to all-the-way winner final furlong when beaten a neck over just short of this trip (good-to-yielding); major player.
runners.comment: Just denied in similar Ballinrobe event recently; obvious claims

runners.name: Divelment
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in maidens at Roscommon last year and again twice over this trip on the AW early this year; overdid the forcing tactics when disappointing in Ballinrobe claimer on latest, claimed afterwards for 5,000euros; definite chance on his best form.
runners.comment: Claimed after disappointing recent Ballinrobe run; definite chance though on overall form

runners.name: Gatlinburg
runners.spotlight: Recent Killarney handicap fourth (1m3f, good) a definite step in the right direction and won't have to improve too much more to play a role here back in ordinary maiden; headgear combination tried.
runners.comment: Killarney handicap fourth won't have to improve too much more to play a role
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing & Mrs A M O'Brien

runners.name: Espritroyale
runners.spotlight: No show in two maidens for Sheila Lavery last year; improved effort in 6f Curragh maiden on debut for this yard in March but took a backward step in Roscommon handicap since; big step up in trip here (half-sister to 1m4f winner).
runners.comment: Bit to find on what we've seen but potential improver now upped in trip

runners.name: Sonny Corleone
runners.spotlight: Shaped encouragingly on second AW outing for Alan McIntyre over the winter, still looking green when fourth over today's trip (Divelment 0.75l ahead in third) in first-time cheekpieces; since joined Gordon Elliott and could go well (cheekpieces left off).
runners.comment: Joined Gordon Elliott since promising Dundalk fourth at this trip; could go well

runners.name: Moon Vega
runners.spotlight: Mild promise on first of two juvenile AW runs last winter; bred to improve with time.
runners.comment: Mild promise on first of two juvenile AW runs last winter; bred to improve with time

runners.name: Cosmic Funk
runners.spotlight: All four runs have been over 7f/1m, showing mild promise in Leopardstown maiden but no improvement dropped to claiming company latest; marked step up in trip here.
runners.comment: All four runs have been over 7f/1m; needs to improve for this step up in trip

runners.name: Tavarua
runners.spotlight: Both runs last month came in soft-ground Gowran maidens over a mile; this longer trip should suit but good bit of improvement required.
runners.comment: This longer trip should suit but good bit of improvement required from two runs

runners.name: Second Line
runners.spotlight: Hasn't troubled the judge in three runs, second occasion not looking to stay 1m2f; needs to improve plenty.
runners.comment: Hasn't troubled the judge in three runs, second occasion not looking to stay 1m2f

runners.name: Amrum
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to Irish 2,000 Guineas winner Siskin; has looked limited in three maiden runs this spring and looks a more likely one for handicaps although this grade of maiden offers possibilities.
runners.comment: Half-brother to Irish Guineas winner Siskin; has looked limited; more one for handicaps

runners.name: Nermal
runners.spotlight: Both runs came over 1m at Dundalk in January, improvement second occasion when made plenty of use of from a wide draw in chasing home a strong favourite; not sure this longer trip will suit but open to more improvement than most and a contender if staying.
runners.comment: Dundalk second open to more improvement than most and a contender if staying

runners.name: Avec Grand Plaisir
runners.spotlight: Just okay runs this term in maidens at Leopardstown and Roscommon; another for whom handicaps beckon.
runners.comment: Okay runs this term in maidens; another for whom handicaps beckon

runners.name: Kingcharles
runners.spotlight: Couple of modest runs of late, likely to need more time and handicaps.
runners.comment: Couple of modest runs of late, likely to need more time and handicaps

races.race_name: Get A Free Month Trial Of Racing TV Now racingtv.com/freetrial Beginners Chase

runners.name: Journey So Far
runners.spotlight: Maiden is rated 106 after four chase runs, the best of which was a 22l seventh in strong Fairyhouse beginners' chase in December (2m, soft, RPR 116); poor twice since over fences and hurdles but big claims on that Fairyhouse run in this company.
runners.comment: Poor the last twice, big chance on December's Fairyhouse beginners' chase run

runners.name: A Snow White Park
runners.spotlight: 66-1 winner on Rules debut in a 4yo hurdle at Ballinrobe (16.5f, good) last May; close fourth in 15.5f novice handicap chase off 102 here on penultimate; well beaten back over hurdles at Downpatrick latest; stamina a query for this trip.
runners.comment: Close fourth in 15.5f novice h'cap chase here on penultimate, stamina to prove

runners.name: Joshua Chamberlain
runners.spotlight: 1-11 over timber and rated 95; back from mammoth layoff with 32l defeat in Punchestown handicap hurdle 16 days ago; sharper now and related to chase winners so could take to this discipline.
runners.comment: Back from huge layoff with 32l defeat in Punchestown h'cap hurdle, bred to make a chaser
runners.owner: J R Robinson & Mrs E Keane

runners.name: Butterfly Mountain
runners.spotlight: Very modest form in two points and three maiden hurdles; best watched on chase debut.
runners.comment: Modest form in points and over hurdles, best watched on chasing debut

runners.name: Are You Busy
runners.spotlight: Chase debutante; 1-8 over hurdles and rated 98; bred to make a chaser and trip and ground should be fine for her here; respectable run over hurdles at Wexford latest (3m, gd-yld); tongue tie goes on; place claims.
runners.comment: 1-8 over hurdles and rated 98, bred to make chaser, tongue tied, place claims

runners.name: Huntsgrove
runners.spotlight: 1-11 over hurdles and rated 100; tailed off in all three previous starts over fences and similar story last two starts in novice hurdles; lacks a recent run and others appeal more.
runners.comment: 100 rated hurdler, tailed off all three chase starts and last twice over hurdles

runners.name: Mount Shenshan
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner is 0-10 over fences; beaten 12.5l off 92 in Wexford handicap chase on latest (25.5f, gd-yld); best form has come on soft and heavy ground; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Hurdles winner, beaten off 92 in h'cap chase latest, best form on softer ground than this

runners.name: Quiet In Bresil
runners.spotlight: Maiden; 0-3 over hurdles and rated 93; never involved on chase debut at Kilbeggan on latest; major improvement required.
runners.comment: Never involved on chase debut at Kilbeggan latest, major improvement required

runners.name: Giant Haystacks
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler is 0-3 in chases and rated 95; kept on well for a 2.75l fourth of 14 in Down Royal beginners' on latest (19.5f, gd-yld); place claims again if sees out this longer trip but that's not guaranteed.
runners.comment: Rated 95, up in trip after keeping on third at Down Royal latest, place claims if stays

runners.name: Parkgarve
runners.spotlight: Promise without winning over hurdles and rated 96 in that sphere; weakened after the third last fence on sole chase outing at Downpatrick (2m2f) last September; has hinted this sort of trip might suit on occasion but much more needed.
runners.comment: 96 rated hurdler, well beaten sole chase start, this trip might suit but more needed

runners.name: Big Island
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler; 0-8 over fences and rated 97; fell when holding every chance in a C&D handicap chase when last seen last August (good); has gone well fresh and not discounted.
runners.comment: Rated 97, fell when in front over C&D when last seen in August, goes well fresh, chance

races.race_name: Tramore Derby July 7th Beginners Chase

runners.name: Mercury Mission
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner with a mark of 113; placed in both completed starts over fences, last time running as well as he was entitled to at Wexford; in the mix.
runners.comment: Three-time hurdle winner with a mark of 113; placed in both completed starts over fences
runners.owner: Paul M Butler & James Patrick Byrne

runners.name: Costacurta
runners.spotlight: Beaten three times under a penalty in novice hurdles after winning his maiden but rated 120 in that scene and he's bred for fences; every chance, for all that he would probably prefer a longer trip.
runners.comment: 120-rated hurdler who is bred for fences and should hold every chance

runners.name: Nelson Muntz
runners.spotlight: Has some useful form over hurdles and on the Flat, though has been tailed off in both disciplines since returning from a break of 72 days; if back on song, he would have a part to play in this company on chase debut.
runners.comment: If back on song he would have a part to play in this company on chase debut

runners.name: Endless Talking
runners.spotlight: Maintained his progress over hurdles with a 20l success from the front in a 2m3f maiden at Downpatrick three weeks ago (good); the RPR he posted reads well in this company and he won a point, so fences shouldn't present a problem.
runners.comment: Goes chasing on the back of winning his maiden hurdle by some 20l

runners.name: Bannow Blaze
runners.spotlight: Consistent hurdler who was in good form for the most part of last year, winning a 2m handicap at Wexford in October; there are successful chasers in the family.
runners.comment: Consistent hurdler who was in good form for the most part of last year

runners.name: Game Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden; inferior hurdler to some in here and she was tailed off in her previous chase.
runners.comment: Inferior hurdler to some in here and she was tailed off in her previous chase
runners.owner: Mrs B Power & John J Phelan

runners.name: Boston Jury
runners.spotlight: Became disappointing over hurdles after winning his maiden and he offered very little on switch to fences last October; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Became disappointing over hurdles and offered very little on switch to fences last October

runners.name: Jerrari
runners.spotlight: Won two good-ground handicap hurdles last summer but hasn't recaptured that level of form this year, including on recent chase debut at Wexford (50-1).
runners.comment: Below his winning hurdle form this year, including on chase debut latest

races.race_name: Walsh King Accountants Handicap Chase

runners.name: Femme Magnifique
runners.spotlight: Bumper and hurdles winner for Willie Mullins in 2023-24; failed to add tom her tally for Paul Kiely last year and off since an okay run at Navan in February; worth a market check on first run for Harry Kelly.
runners.comment: On long losing run, okay run at Navan in Feb', off since, new yard, check market

runners.name: Itsalonglongroad
runners.spotlight: Veteran; five-time chase winner including three over C&D; last won here last August off 99 and 3lb lower now; recent efforts have been underwhelming, however, and blinkers are reached for.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner, 3lb below last win but out of form, blinkers tried

runners.name: Presenting Lad
runners.spotlight: Two chase wins in 2024 (including a novice over C&D) but it has been slim pickings since then; no joy in 2025 and modest run at Wexford on return from a break on latest; on a dangerous mark but needs a revival.
runners.comment: C&D winner in 2024, out of form last year, below best after break latest, revival needed

runners.name: Ballybrittas
runners.spotlight: Point and Limerick maiden hurdle winner (16.5f, good) has finished runner up in two of his seven starts over fences; beaten 17.5l on last two starts at Kilbeggan and Killarney at around 2m4f; drop back in trip here a plus; rated 10lb higher over timber; could go well.
runners.comment: Decent efforts last twice over further, drop in trip a plus, 10lb higher over hurdles

runners.name: Lady Doyanne
runners.spotlight: Wexford maiden hurdle winner last August (2m1f, gd-yld); not a bad run in handicap hurdle at Clonmel latest; turns her hand to chasing now and pedigree suggests she'll take to this game; yard in form; interesting.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner, chase debut, bred for this game, yard in form, interesting

runners.name: Bynx
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner (including here) is 1-4 over fences; that win came at Wexford (2m, good) off 7lb lower last August; out of depth in Listed hurdle latest but this more suitable and she goes well here; not out of this.
runners.comment: Course winner over hurdles, 1-4 over fences, this more suitable than latest, chance

runners.name: Stuntman Steve
runners.spotlight: Two-time hurdles winner is 0-5 in chases; out of form in 2026 but has yet to get his favoured good ground; could go well in first-time cheekpieces with conditions to suit.
runners.comment: 0-5 over fences, will appreciate the return to good ground, cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Polly Poppins
runners.spotlight: Best two runs of her career have come here; beaten a head in 16.5f (good) handicap hurdle last August; 3.25l third in C&D handicap chase when last seen in October and 1lb lower now; could go well if ready after 232 day absence.
runners.comment: Best runs here, 3.25l third in C&D h'cap chase here in Oct', go well if fit after break

runners.name: Be Fierce
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles at Punchestown in January (2m, sft-hvy); his C&D novice chase win came on testing ground too and it was yielding when he won here over hurdles; goes well fresh so lack of a recent run isn't a concern but could do with some rain.
runners.comment: Course winner over hurdles (yld) and fences (hvy), goes well fresh but ground a worry

runners.name: Kingdom Calling
runners.spotlight: Shock Listowel maiden hurdle winner in August 2024; hasn't come close to replicating that form since and leap of faith required to fancy him on his chase debut; 3lb wrong.
runners.comment: No impact since shock Listowel win in 2024, chase debut, leap of faith required; 3lb wrong

runners.name: Party President
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back races over hurdles last year but lost form subsequently and has since left Tony Mullins; off since January and best watched on stable and chase debut.
runners.comment: Lost form since back-to-back hurdles win last year, new yard, chase debut, watch

runners.name: Typical Thomas
runners.spotlight: Two-time chase winner but losing run stretches back to October 2023; off since okay run on the Flat at Listowel last September; poor record when fresh; others preferred.
runners.comment: On long losing run, off since last September, poor record fresh, others preferred

races.race_name: Success Days At Kilbarry Lodge Stud Mares Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Poetic Twist
runners.spotlight: Receiving weight off the winner when beaten a neck at Killarney (2m6f maiden) but no disgrace in that effort and the front two were clear; strong claims.
runners.comment: Credentials are clear on the strength of her near miss in a maiden at Killarney

runners.name: Luciole Des Bordes
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two bumpers before racing too freely and not getting home in the latest at Clonmel (2m4f, yielding); no surprise to see a hood turned to and major player if giving herself a chance.
runners.comment: Second in two bumpers and raced too freely in the latest; now hooded

runners.name: Muted Melody
runners.spotlight: Some promise in bumpers; got behind early (started slowly) before staying on into third in an 18-runner maiden at Clonmel (2m, good to yielding) and that's something to build on; blinkers join the usual hood.
runners.comment: Stayed on into third in a 2m maiden at Clonmel; up in trip with blinkers added

runners.name: Ice Breeze Baby
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to point winner That's Me; dam was fourth in a point and related to winning jumpers; the market can guide on debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister to point winner That's Me; the market can guide on debut

runners.name: Giorgia Rose
runners.spotlight: Competitive in points but was a tailed-off last in a Clonmel bumper, in which a below par Luciole Des Bordes was ahead of her; the tongue-tie tried then is now joined by cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Competitive in points but was a tailed-off last in a Clonmel bumper; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Straightupsideways
runners.spotlight: Beaten when falling in her two points and makes limited appeal on hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Beaten when falling in her two points and makes limited appeal on hurdle debut

runners.name: Mysilverriverfeale
runners.spotlight: Some signs of progress when beaten 18l in a 2m6f maiden at Killarney only recently but she'll need more again.
runners.comment: Some signs of progress when beaten 18l in a 2m6f maiden at Killarney only recently

runners.name: Northernlightsky
runners.spotlight: Modest point form and hasn't shown a lot more in three bumpers.
runners.comment: Modest point form and hasn't shown a lot more in three bumpers

runners.name: Howyasox
runners.spotlight: No obvious claims on what she's shown in points, two bumpers and a maiden hurdle.
runners.comment: No obvious claims on what she's shown in points, two bumpers and a maiden hurdle

runners.name: Rebel Queen
runners.spotlight: Bit better when beaten about 11l in a Limerick maiden but others have achieved plenty more.
runners.comment: Bit better when beaten about 11l in a Limerick maiden but others have achieved plenty more

races.race_name: Tramore Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Tedworth
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win here (20.5f, yielding) off 6lb lower last month and ran respectably off revised mark when a 5l fourth of 15 at Clonmel on latest (19.5f, good); place claims.
runners.comment: Won over 20.5f here on penultimate, solid fourth off revised mark latest, place claims

runners.name: Miss Donna
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; glimmers of ability in maiden hurdles including here; weakened over 2m1f (gd-yld) in Wexford handicap hurdle latest; not sure this longer trip will suit on that evidence.
runners.comment: Weakened over 2m1f at Wexford latest, this longer trip is a query; reserve

runners.name: Katie's Casper
runners.spotlight: No show in maidens or when backed into 15-2 from 14-1 on handicap debut at Downpatrick latest but may have needed that after a break and yard has hit form; don't discount.
runners.comment: No impact on h'cap debut latest but may have needed that, yard in form, can't discount

runners.name: Zolpharine
runners.spotlight: Impressive handicap chase win at Limerick on latest (22.5f, good); now looks to exploit 12lb lower hurdles mark but hasn't won in this sphere since September 2023; in form, though, and can't discount.
runners.comment: Easy chase win latest, 12lb lower over hurdles, in form and can't be discounted

runners.name: Soldier Saint
runners.spotlight: Placed in a bumper for Nicky Henderson; best effort yet for this yard an 11l fifth of 15 at Clonmel (19.5f, gd-yld) latest; this trip might suit on that evidence and O'Keefe up now; interesting.
runners.comment: Best run yet for this yard latest, shaped like this trip might suit, O'Keefe up, chance

runners.name: Maxicourt
runners.spotlight: Standout effort came at this venue (2m, yielding) last October when 1 2.25l second to Cousin Kate; hasn't replicated that since and was reluctant at the start at Cork on latest in cheekpieces (discarded); a bit to prove up in trip.
runners.comment: Best run came over 2m here in October, hasn't repeated it since, a bit to prove for now

runners.name: Clever Court
runners.spotlight: Huge odds and well beaten in maidens; pulled up on last three start; blinkers replace cheekpieces; others preferred.
runners.comment: Huge odds and no impact in maidens, pulled up last thrice, blinkers on, others preferred

runners.name: Brianna Lily
runners.spotlight: No win since 2023 but step in the right direction when a keeping on 4.75l third at Clonmel (19.5f, gd-yld) 15 days ago; effectively 7lb lower here with rider's claim; stays this far and in the mix with a repeat of that last effort.
runners.comment: Back to form on latest at Clonmel, rider takes off handy 5lb, stays this far, in the mix

runners.name: Kilbarry Ce Ce
runners.spotlight: 0-12; best runs have come at this venue but over shorter trips than this; could run into a place if her stamina holds out (not certain on breeding).
runners.comment: 0-12, best form has come here over shorter, place chance if stamina holds out
runners.owner: Mrs Con O'Keeffe & Tadhg O'Sullivan & Anthony O'Keeffe

runners.name: Bite That
runners.spotlight: Last won over hurdles here (2m, good) in 2022; rated 23lb higher over fences but hard to fancy on the evidence of his last three runs over hurdles at Clonmel.
runners.comment: Course winner in 2022 (2m), rated 15lb higher over fences, recent hurdles form poor

runners.name: Set The Tone
runners.spotlight: Maiden; yet to deliver on promise shown in 3yo hurdles in 2024; badly out off form over fences and hurdles in 2026; not easy to make a case for.
runners.comment: Maiden, badly out of form over fences and hurdles in 2026, hard to make a case for

runners.name: U Asking Me
runners.spotlight: Last won at Clonmel in October 2024 off 4lb lower (2m4f, good); posted a decent effort in a handicap chase when last seen at Wexford last July; likely to need this run.
runners.comment: No win since October 2024, off since decent chase run last July, might need this run

runners.name: Cruisin Susan
runners.spotlight: Has lost way since a purple patch in 2024 which included a win here over 20.5f (gd-yld) off 15lb higher; not a bad run on return from 133 days off latest and on a dangerous mark if she's back on song.
runners.comment: Course winner in 2024, not a bad run on return from break latest, on dangerous mark

runners.name: Just For One
runners.spotlight: Bounced back from being pulled up in successive races with a 16.5f (gd-yld) win here off 5lb lower last month; bred to stay this far and leading chance if backing that last run up.
runners.comment: Won off 5lb lower here on latest at 16.5f, bred to stay, big chance if in same form

runners.name: Not So Serious
runners.spotlight: First reserve; very modest form in maiden hurdles; glimmers of promise in both bumper runs so could improve for switch to handicaps; check market.
runners.comment: No show in maidens but hinted at ability in bumpers, check market; reserve

runners.name: Strand Street Lass
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; soundly beaten in completed maiden hurdles; pulled up the last twice; blinkers go on for handicap debut; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Handicap debutante, shown nothing in maidens, blinkers on, hard to fancy; reserve

runners.name: Friends Of Barry
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in four maiden hurdles at 2m-2m7f on varying ground; cheekpieces go on for handicap debut; can only be watched.
runners.comment: Poor form in maiden hurdles, cheekpieces on for handicap debut, best watched

runners.name: Evening's Empire
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 0-11 over timber; pulled up over C&D on last start in this sphere; tailed off on Flat at Leopardstown latest; Brianna Lily looks yard's best chance.
runners.comment: 0-11 over hurdles, tailed off on Flat latest, Brianna Lily looks yard's best chance

runners.name: Captain P
runners.spotlight: Point winner is 0-6 over hurdles and yet to trouble the judge; beaten 21l on last handicap hurdle start and 25l behind the winner in Sligo beginners' chase when last seen in October.
runners.comment: 0-6 over hurdles, yet to trouble judge, off since poor chase run in Oct', hard to fancy

races.race_name: Graun Hill Suite Hospitality Packages At tramoreraces.ie Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Playtime
runners.spotlight: 1m4f winner on good ground; drew clear to win a Leopardstown maiden in March (2m, soft to heavy) but found wanting in handicaps since then, proving disappointing when sights were lowered at Killarney 20 days ago.
runners.comment: Proved disappointing when sights were lowered at Killarney 20 days ago

runners.name: Eastmore
runners.spotlight: Held his form well after winning a Down Royal handicap last August (2m1f, good to firm); back from a break he appeared to get outstayed by the front two over 2m3f at Cork; better expected here.
runners.comment: Consistent 6yo who appeared to get outstayed when third over 2m3f at Cork latest

runners.name: Doyen Magic
runners.spotlight: Won her last race over hurdles on heavy ground at Limerick in November and twice runner-up over fences since then; however, last week's run at Limerick needs forgiving and may prefer softer ground.
runners.comment: Last week's chase run at Limerick needs forgiving and may prefer softer ground

runners.name: Splashing Out
runners.spotlight: Hurdle/chase winner; had lost her way but off a reduced mark she rediscovered some form when beaten about 6l into third at Downpatrick three weeks ago (2m2f, good); won't be far away if as good again.
runners.comment: Off a reduced mark she rediscovered some form to finish third at Downpatrick

runners.name: The Monkey Pole
runners.spotlight: Worryingly below par in his last two races (bumper/maiden) but his seconds in maidens last August (one here) gives him definite hopes off this sort of mark; hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Below par in last two races but has the form to feature off this sort of mark

runners.name: Stepdance
runners.spotlight: Won three times at up to 1m4f at a modest level last year; her middle effort in maidens reads well with a view to this initial mark and she's considered.
runners.comment: Flat winner; her middle effort in maidens reads well with a view to this initial mark

runners.name: Figero
runners.spotlight: It was a modest maiden he won at Kilbeggan but he appreciated the good to yielding ground, having been faced with soft on his hurdle debut; unexposed handicap debutant.
runners.comment: It was a modest maiden he won at Kilbeggan but he's an unexposed handicap debutant

races.race_name: August Festival Early Bird Tickets At tramoreraces.ie (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: One Day In May
runners.spotlight: Form figures of 2221 in points and kept on for third when hooded in a bumper at Wexford (good to yielding); that form entitles her to a leading role here.
runners.comment: Point winner and third in a Wexford bumper; looks set to play a leading role here

runners.name: Highlander Addict
runners.spotlight: Beaten 11l into second at Navan but was closer at Punchestown in a stronger bumper than this, on yielding ground; looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: Second at Navan and closer again at Punchestown in a stronger bumper than this

runners.name: Goodmancon
runners.spotlight: Ran well to be third in a Tramore bumper in April but fared less well at Killarney and he's been beaten in nine bumpers already.
runners.comment: Placed in a maiden hurdle but already beaten in nine bumpers

runners.name: Maptite Chipie
runners.spotlight: By a useful French sire but cost only 2,500euros as a 2yo; likely one to watch.
runners.comment: By a useful French sire but cost only 2,500euros as a 2yo; likely one to watch

runners.name: Game Shadow
runners.spotlight: Out of a 2m hurdle winner and sister to useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Baltiman; a bit ominous to see a hood applied from the outset.
runners.comment: Out of a 2m hurdle winner; a bit ominous to see a hood applied from the outset

runners.name: Lincoln Mill
runners.spotlight: Clear of the third when runner-up in a maiden point at Dromahane recently; his future probably lies over further.
runners.comment: Clear of the third when runner-up in a maiden point at Dromahane recently

runners.name: Top Of The League
runners.spotlight: Just minor promise in his five points and wouldn't be the obvious answer to this bumper.
runners.comment: Just minor promise in his five points and wouldn't be the obvious answer to this bumper

runners.name: Talas Kera
runners.spotlight: Ordinary bumper form and her third on hurdle debut was remote and in a small field; pulled up since then.
runners.comment: Her third on hurdle debut was remote and in a small field; pulled up since then

runners.name: Blaze Of Honour
runners.spotlight: Never dangerous when beaten 30l into fifth in a good-ground bumper at Sligo; remains best watched.
runners.comment: Never dangerous when beaten 30l into fifth in a good-ground bumper at Sligo

runners.name: Oops A Daisy
runners.spotlight: Finished completely tailed off in a bumper at Killarney only recently when a 40-1 chance.
runners.comment: Finished completely tailed off in a bumper at Killarney only recently when a 40-1 chance

runners.name: Distraction
runners.spotlight: Went off 80-1 when pulled up in a maiden hurdle at Cork in November (yielding to soft).
runners.comment: Went off 80-1 when pulled up in a maiden hurdle at Cork in November (yielding to soft)

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Pleasant Man
runners.spotlight: On a losing run and he beat only one in 11.5f Lingfield handicap ten days ago; not ruled out though if back on his A-game.
runners.comment: Beat only one at Lingfield ten days ago; not ruled out though if back on his A-game

runners.name: Beaune
runners.spotlight: In fine form on the AW in 2026, scoring twice over C&D in April; beat one back on turf at Bath last time but no surprise to see him get back on track here.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner in April; beat one at Bath latest but can get back on track here

runners.name: I'd Go Maniac
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in November; got back on track in first-time cheekpieces when second of 13 in 1m2f Windsor handicap 18 days ago; can go well again back up in trip with the headgear retained.
runners.comment: C&D winner; cheekpieces on when second at Windsor latest; can go well again

runners.name: Arth's Gold
runners.spotlight: Finally opened his account at Southwell in April and he followed it with a solid third of eight there eight days ago; ought to be thereabouts again.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell; followed it with solid third there eight days ago; in the mix again

runners.name: Glitter Code
runners.spotlight: Remains winless but he shaped well in first-time blinkers for his new yard when second of eight in 8.5f handicap at Beverley last month; can go well again in retained headgear if his stamina holds out over this much longer trip.
runners.comment: Bright start for yard with Beverley 2nd (8.5f) latest; can go well if stamina holds out

runners.name: Relocal
runners.spotlight: Scored at Chelmsford (1m5f) for Roger Varian in January; not disgraced for his new yard with third of six in 1m6f Yarmouth handicap nine days ago, so he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Not disgraced for new yard with third at Yarmouth nine days ago; considered

runners.name: Jack Sparowe
runners.spotlight: Blinkered when ending a lengthy losing sequence in good style at Yarmouth nine days ago, drawing clear last 1f; up 6lb but another bold showing is on the cards with headgear retained and good amateur Henry Callan up.
runners.comment: Blinkered when ending a lengthy losing sequence in good style at Yarmouth; big player

runners.name: Someone's Wish
runners.spotlight: Course scorer (for Robert Eddery) in 2023; a fair winning hurdler/chaser for Gordon Elliott since but pulled up over hurdles at Perth last month on final run for yard; more needed for his new stable.
runners.comment: Fair winning hurdler/chaser at best for Gordon Elliott; more needed here for new yard

runners.name: Green Team
runners.spotlight: A three-time winner (including over C&D) in 2025 for Ian Williams; in good nick for his present yard too, fourth of eight in 1m3f Southwell handicap eight days ago; not out of things.
runners.comment: In good nick for his present yard, fourth at Southwell eight days ago; not out of things

runners.name: Lenny's Spirit
runners.spotlight: In good form on the AW at Lingfield this year until a below-par sixth on turf in 1m2f Windsor handicap 18 days ago; this course winner can bounce back here.
runners.comment: In good form on the AW until sixth on turf at Windsor; this course winner can bounce back

runners.name: Sisterandbrother
runners.spotlight: Has made a bright start for his current yard and made it two wins from his last three runs in 1m4f handicap at Beverley two days ago; also took this in 2024, so he's a player under a 4lb penalty.
runners.comment: Bright start for current yard and scored at Beverley two days ago; took this in 2024 too

runners.name: Now Then Wendy
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m4f AW winner for Hughie Morrison in 2023; seemed only poor over hurdles for Steph Cameron subsequently, pulled up at Uttoxeter 27 days ago; returns to this sphere for her new handler with something to prove.
runners.comment: Poor over hurdles for Steph Cameron of late; returns to this sphere for new handler

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Always Happy
runners.spotlight: Siyouni filly who got off to a winning start in 1m Southwell novice in March; beaten at odds-on but advanced her form when second of 12 under a penalty in 1m Lingfield novice 17 days ago; she holds very good form claims.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell; good second at Lingfield 17 days ago; holds very good form claims
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Venetian Sky
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica filly who took a big step forward from her debut when a running-on third of six in 7f Yarmouth novice 29 days ago; she's open to further progress, especially over this longer distance.
runners.comment: Running-on third at Yarmouth 29 days ago; open to further progress over this longer trip

runners.name: Hatour
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly; has shaped with promise on both her runs, running on well late on when third of nine in 7f Lingfield novice on her return 17 days ago; this step up in trip promises to suit; player.
runners.comment: Ran on well when third at Lingfield latest; player with this step up in trip sure to suit

runners.name: Hollywell Stream
runners.spotlight: 13-2, promising neck second of six in maiden at Doncaster (7f, soft) on her debut in October; winner has gone in again since, so this Lope De Vega filly must enter calculations with Oisin Murphy in the saddle.
runners.comment: Neck second of six in maiden at Doncaster on her debut in October; must enter calculations

runners.name: Moonlit Surf
runners.spotlight: Oasis Dream filly; still looked green but improved when sixth of ten in 1m Windsor maiden 11 days ago; can do better still, especially once handicapping.
runners.comment: Stepped up on debut when sixth at Windsor latest; can do better still once handicapping

runners.name: Flagpole
runners.spotlight: 15-2, encouraging debut fourth of 14 in 1m Southwell novice in November; this Starspangledbanner filly seems sure to build on it after a break.
runners.comment: Encouraging debut fourth at Southwell in November; seems sure to build on it after break

races.race_name: Free Tips On attheraces.com Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Harlequin Sky
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to six winners, the best being In The Breeze (1m2f/1m4f; RPR 91); backed at double-figure odds on debut, when green but keeping on well to be under 3l third at Yarmouth (5f, good) last week; promising start; step up in trip should suit; contender.
runners.comment: Made a promising start at Yarmouth (5f, good); step up in trip should suit; contender.

runners.name: Angel Sense
runners.spotlight: Showed signs of greenness when a close second at Kempton (6f, Polytack) and when beaten a similar distance in third at Nottingham (6f, good; favourite) where she improved; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Beaten under 1l on both starts (both 6f) and improved; not ruled out.

runners.name: Zarakova
runners.spotlight: 190,000gns yearling by Zarak; first foal; dam Group-placed 5f 2yo winner (RPR 99), half-sister to Canadian 1m Grade 2 winner Elizabeth Way, out of 1m Group 1 winner; leading stable is 11% with AW 2yos (five seasons) but 1-3 with them this term; market check suggested.
runners.comment: Dam Group-placed 5f 2yo winner; trainer 11% with AW 2yos (five seasons); market check.

runners.name: Rosevannion
runners.spotlight: 82,000gns yearling; seventh foal; Zoustar half-sister to winners Saariselka (6f 2yo; RPR 89), Israaj (1m/1m1f including 2yo/dirt/KSA; 84) and Kohoof (Spanish 6f-1m); dam unraced half-sister to two sprint winners; trainer 18% with AW 2yos (five seasons); market check suggested.
runners.comment: Half-sister to three winners (best RPR 89); trainer 18% with AW 2yos (five seasons).
runners.owner: C Campbell, Ballylinch Stud & Brook Stud

runners.name: Anad
runners.spotlight: 150,000euros yearling; fourth foal; State Of Rest half-sister to winners Miami Matrix (7f/7.5f including AW; RPR 90), Ob La Di (10.3f/12.3f; 89) and Baltic Fleet (1m1f 2yo; 88); dam French Listed-placed 1m2f/1m3f winner (95); powerful stable is 2-4 with 2yos on AW this year; interesting.
runners.comment: Half-sister to three winners (best RPR 90); trainer 2-4 with AW 2yos in 2026; interesting.

runners.name: Mohaymenah
runners.spotlight: By Minzaal; first foal; dam 6f winner (including 2yo; RPR 93); 11-1 at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) four weeks ago when racing prominently before weakening; looked likely to be suited by this slightly longer trip; needs to take a good step up on that effort to be involved.
runners.comment: 4l fourth on debut at Doncaster (6f); step up in trip looks positive; improvement needed.

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Night Mission
runners.spotlight: Second or third in his three runs in 2yo novice events (all 6f, good ground or slower); unplaced in two 6f handicaps this year but he drops in class today and his pedigree strongly suggests stepping up to 7f will help; finished behind Dublin Bay at Ascot last time but was racing away from the main action; well drawn and a return to more positive tactics could see him run a big race.
runners.comment: Unplaced in both handicaps this year but now up in trip and down in class; interesting

runners.name: Paranjape
runners.spotlight: 6f maiden win here last November; stayed on for second of 12 in a Southwell handicap last month (6f); that race is yet to throw up a subsequent winner (as of before racing on Thursday) and he himself has since failed to fire at Haydock; stamina not assured.
runners.comment: Course winner (6f); below par on turf latest; stamina not assured
runners.owner: G H Thompson & S P C Woods

runners.name: Wyle Cop
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a 6f maiden here on his reappearance in March; fair third at Southwell later that month but his form has nosedived on turf (handled it fine as a 2yo) on his last two starts (both in cheekpieces which are absent today); hopes pinned on the step up to 7f and the return to AW sparking a revival.
runners.comment: Two good AW runs in March but poor on turf on last two runs; headgear now off; up in trip

runners.name: Chapman's Peak
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a pair of wins in Kempton nurseries (1m and 7f); he has struggled in two handicaps this time around, however, and comes here with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 on the up but he has failed to fire in two runs this year; has a point to prove

runners.name: Enamorus
runners.spotlight: She didn't quite live up to market expectation in three turf runs as a 2yo but there were clear signs of encouragement with her three in-frame efforts; not obviously thrown in for this handicap/seasonal debut but the trainer is in form and Oisin Murphy is always a significant booking for the stable (57 wins and a further 63 second/third place finishes from 217 rides; level-stake profit of +37); market confidence would heighten interest.
runners.comment: Didn't live up to market billing as a 2yo but still showed clear promise; can do better

runners.name: Comprador
runners.spotlight: Consistent without progressing in four AW runs (6f) for Ed Walker this year; changed hands for 16,000gns last month; improvement for today's extra furlong is essential.
runners.comment: Consistent in four 6f AW runs for E Walker; sold 16,000gns since latest; new trip today

runners.name: Inferno
runners.spotlight: No improvement for the switch to handicaps at Nottingham three weeks ago (8.3f, good), unable to land a blow; a tongue-tie and blinkers are now added but he is drawn wide and hasn't looked to be crying out for the drop to 7f.
runners.comment: Low-key handicap debut latest; drops in trip with accessories fitted; tough draw

runners.name: Court Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Had issues with the stalls as a 2yo but she has returned from a winter break as an improved performer, winning a 7f handicap at Kempton this month and failing narrowly over C&D 11 days ago; should still have more to offer; one of two live chances for Ollie Sangster.
runners.comment: Improved in AW handicaps this year, winning at Kempton then second over C&D; more to come

runners.name: Dublin Bay
runners.spotlight: Improved for the switch to nurseries last summer, winning easily at Ffos Las and Doncaster (both 6f) before finishing off strongly for sixth in a much stronger race at York's Ebor meeting; better than ever when winning a C&D handicap in April and his subsequent fifth of 19 at Ascot was in a Class 4; could still rate higher; key player despite a wider draw than ideal.
runners.comment: C&D win last month; creditable fifth in big field at Ascot latest; respected once more

runners.name: Horwich
runners.spotlight: Won his first two starts, both over C&D, when trained by Clive Cox; struggled initially in handicaps for Ruth Carr but having dropped 10lb in the weights there were more encouraging signs over C&D 11 days ago (nearly 2l behind Court Of Stars), travelling strongly but just tapped for speed early in the straight; another 2lb lower here and he now has something to build on.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins for C Cox; first promise for new yard when fourth here 11 days ago; chance

runners.name: Flyta
runners.spotlight: She was not beaten far in a pair of Kempton fillies' maidens (1m and 7f) in January, improving for the addition of a tongue-tie; neither race appeals as strong form though and she looks up against it now handicapping from the widest stall.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in two fillies' maidens at Kempton in January; tough task from wide draw

races.race_name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Alondra
runners.spotlight: Has a good C&D record which includes two wins in February, and there wasn't much she could do about her latest defeat on this track, when third to one well ahead of his mark; down in grade and one to consider in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Has a good C&D record; down in grade from latest run here; first-time headgear

runners.name: Dark Kestrel
runners.spotlight: C&D winner whose five AW wins include two this year; often slowly away; has been running respectably off his lower turf mark of late but probably needs a bit more for the adding of a visor.
runners.comment: C&D winner; two 5f wins this year; has share of weight; needs to find more for new visor

runners.name: True Promise
runners.spotlight: Four wins on Tapeta elsewhere, at 5f-7f, all in 2024; has been involved in several tight finishes since December, including for Oisin Murphy at Kempton (6f) in April, and while he's been on much the same mark throughout, he's worth another go over a bare 5f; thereabouts.
runners.comment: Four wins on Tapeta elsewhere in 2024; many close calls since December; thereabouts

runners.name: Ancient State
runners.spotlight: Looked ahead of his mark in first three 5f wins on the AW, most recently at Southwell in March; missed a beat at the start and only just failed to reel in the more prominently ridden Spendmore Lane at Lingfield since; raised a further 3lb but still has potential.
runners.comment: Looked ahead of his mark in first three AW wins; just failed against Spendmore Lane latest

runners.name: Spendmore Lane
runners.spotlight: Often leads but can be slowly away; took a steep rise in the weights for two 5f wins in early spring but she absorbed it and added another win, from the fast-finishing Ancient State, on Lingfield AW in April; another solid effort back on turf there 11 days ago; strong showing likely back at 5f on AW.
runners.comment: Three AW wins at 5f in 2026, including C&D; solid 3rd on turf latest; strong bid likely

runners.name: Jojo Rabbit
runners.spotlight: Four C&D wins but the latest was back in 2024 and he's struggled a bit here off this sort of mark in recent attempts; has been running well on turf of late, though, and he shouldn't be that far away.
runners.comment: Four C&D wins; vulnerable in recent attempts here but has been running well on turf
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing XIII & Ruth Carr

runners.name: Mick's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Cracking 8yo who has won two Polytrack races in every season for the last five years; however, he finished behind Spendmore Lane and Ancient State at Lingfield recently and the return to Tapeta looks a negative.
runners.comment: Multiple wins on Polytrack; held in 4th at Lingfield latest; surface query back on Tapeta

runners.name: Hedge Fund
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f on this track as 2yo; winless since that season but he's shown enough to think he can be placed to advantage off a reduced mark; had a troubled start to the season at Beverley but it's too soon to write him off; hood refitted with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Won over 6f here as 2yo; winless since that season but well handicapped now

runners.name: Blue Force
runners.spotlight: Has won on all three Tapeta tracks, most recently over C&D in December, not as good since and although he ran better back here in April, he found little at Newcastle next time; others more likely.
runners.comment: C&D winner in December; not as good since; well held at Newcastle latest
runners.owner: John And Tony Jarvis And Partner

runners.name: Curious Rover
runners.spotlight: Useful 5f turf form in 2025 but his season slowed down later in the year and he's been below best, despite the reduced mark, this term; 0-3 on Newcastle AW but he did handle that Tapeta earlier in his career, so perhaps the switch to this track might pay off in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Useful 5f turf form as 4yo; below best for a while now; 0-3 on AW; first-time headgear
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

races.race_name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Galilean Quality
runners.spotlight: Rounded off his 2yo campaign with a game 1m2f win at Pontefract (soft), after which he was gelded; made all to beat his sole opponent in a Southwell match (1m4f) last month but he could manage only third behind that reopposing rival at Chester (a solid effort nevertheless) three weeks ago; still capable of better; trainer also saddles High Storm.
runners.comment: Only third at Chester last time but still a rock-solid effort; Tapeta winner; contender

runners.name: Mythical Bay
runners.spotlight: Made heavy weather of it when landing a 9.4f novice here in January (2-5 favourite); better in form terms when second of eight in a Chester handicap three weeks ago (extended 1m4f, good; Galilean Quality third) but he looked a tricky ride in the straight, hanging both ways; has the ability to deal with a 3lb rise but there are reservations.
runners.comment: Good form when 2nd at Chester last time but he didn't look straightforward; may do better

runners.name: High Storm
runners.spotlight: Disappointed as favourite in a 1m2f handicap at Newmarket last month but his earlier Doncaster win (1m2f, good to soft) was encouraging; promise on AW in February; should stay 1m4f and he is not fully exposed just yet; Karl Burke is also responsible for Galilean Quality.
runners.comment: Disappointing favourite at Newmarket Craven meeting (1m2f); should stay; trainer runs two

runners.name: Baltic Fleet
runners.spotlight: Narrowly landed a 1m1f maiden at Yarmouth last September and he ran to a similar level when second at Bath on his reappearance last month (1m2f, good); gelded prior to his handicap debut at York (1m4f) two weeks ago, a race in which he was never far away but looked a bit awkward under pressure; upped 2lb but not yet exposed; AW debut.
runners.comment: Improved for 1m4f/handicaps when 3rd at York latest; up 2lb but can do better; AW debut
